summary: Triage remaining expense-audit findings, design + adversarially review each, return vetted implementation packages
logs: Triage found 52 remaining findings; designing top 14.

item.title: Import never sets currency/exchange_rate/amount_base — imported expenses default to USD/1.0/NULL
item.scope: Add a BEFORE INSERT trigger populating amount_base=amount*exchange_rate (and tax_amount_base) when NULL so no write path leaves them unset; add currency/exchange_rate to import config and route import through createExpense/RPC.

design.title: Import never sets currency/exchange_rate/amount_base — defensive DB trigger + correct import config
design.summary:
The expense import path is a "dumb" batch INSERT (ImportWizard.tsx handleImport, lines 174-293) that builds records purely from mapped CSV columns and inserts straight into `expenses` via a loose-typed client. It never runs the rate-snapshot logic that `createExpense` (expensesService.ts:192-224) does, so imported rows fall to the live DB defaults verified this session: `currency 'USD'`, `exchange_rate 1`, `rate_source 'derived'`, and `amount_base`/`tax_amount_base` NULL. `getExpenseStats` (expensesService.ts:671-672) and `getExpensesByCategory` then treat every imported foreign-currency expense as already-base at rate 1 via the `amount_base ?? amount*exchange_rate` fallback, silently corrupting cross-currency totals for any non-base-currency lab. `ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses` (importExportService.ts:160-175) also omits currency/exchange_rate/tax_amount/amount_base entirely and lists a phantom `paid_at` dateField (no such column — overlaps EXP-065).

The audit's verdict (line 746) is the spine of this design: adding config columns alone is NOT sufficient because the dumb INSERT still won't compute `amount_base`. The robust, write-path-agnostic fix is a BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE trigger on `expenses` that populates `amount_base`/`tax_amount_base` from `amount`/`tax_amount` × `exchange_rate` whenever they are NULL — so NO write path (import, future RPC, raw SQL, legacy backfill) can leave them unset. It mirrors the verified server-side helpers `_fin_base_currency(tenant)` and `_fin_currency_decimals(code)` and the `convertToBase = round(amount*rate, baseDecimals)` semantics already used by createExpense. Because createExpense/updateExpense already compute and pass `amount_base`, the trigger is a no-op for them (fills only when NULL) — zero behavior change on the form path. We then complete `ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses` so importers can supply currency/exchange_rate/tax_amount, remove the phantom `paid_at`, and add a one-line in-wizard note. This is the SAFE, fully-additive slice; the larger "route import through an atomic approve+post RPC / force pending" ambition (EXP-031/063/064) is explicitly deferred.

files.change: Create SECURITY DEFINER fn set_expenses_base_amounts() (plpgsql, SET search_path=public): resolve base currency via _fin_base_currency(NEW.tenant_id), decimals via _fin_currency_decimals(base); if NEW.amount_base IS NULL set it to round(COALESCE(NEW.amount,0)*COALESCE(NEW.exchange_rate,1), dec); if NEW.tax_amount_base IS NULL set it to round(COALESCE(NEW.tax_amount,0)*COALESCE(NEW.exchange_rate,1), dec). Fills only when NULL so service-computed values are never overwritten. Attach trigger named zz_set_expenses_base_amounts BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE — the zz_ prefix guarantees it fires AFTER set_expenses_tenant_and_audit (Postgres orders same-timing row triggers alphabetically), so NEW.tenant_id is populated before base resolution. Includes one-time additive backfill of NULL base rows scoped to deleted_at IS NULL. REVOKE ALL on the fn from public. Full SQL in migrationSql. Main loop must regenerate database.types.ts and update the migration manifest after applying.

Fix ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses (lines 160-175): dateFields -> ['expense_date','approved_at'] (REMOVE phantom paid_at — no such column; overlaps EXP-065). numberFields -> ['amount','tax_amount','exchange_rate'] so imported tax/rate values are number-coerced and the trigger can compute base from them. Add optional stringFields?: string[] to the EntityConfig type (default []) and set stringFields:['currency','vendor','reference','notes'] for expenses so 'currency' becomes a mappable target (currency is free-text, flows through ImportWizard's generic non-empty-string branch on insert). Do NOT add amount_base/tax_amount_base to any list — never importable; the trigger computes them. Leave uniqueFields as-is (DB unique index is EXP-023/EXP-065).

(1) Line 605 dropdown union: append ...(config.stringFields ?? []) so currency/vendor/reference become selectable target fields for non-inventory entities. (2) Add an expenses-only inline advisory using the existing bg-info-muted border-info/30 info-box tokens already in this file (around the Step 1 required-fields panel, lines 438-450): 'Include currency and exchange_rate columns for non-base-currency expenses. If omitted, rows are stored in your base currency at rate 1.0.' Tokens only, no raw colors; no other logic change to the batch insert.

New Vitest unit test. (1) convertToBase/roundMoney invariant cases mirroring the SQL trigger: EUR 1000 @ 0.42 base OMR(3dp) -> 420.000; rate=1 identity; tax_amount 0 -> 0; JPY(0dp) integer rounding — catches TS<->SQL rounding drift for the positive-amount domain. (2) Config-shape guards: assert ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses.dateFields excludes 'paid_at', numberFields includes 'tax_amount' and 'exchange_rate', and stringFields includes 'currency'. First expense/import test file in the repo.

design.tests: src/lib/__tests__/expenseImportBase.test.ts (new): (1) convertToBase invariant cases — EUR1000@0.42 base OMR(3dp)->420.000; rate=1 identity; tax 0->0; JPY(0dp) integer rounding — mirror exactly what the SQL trigger computes, catching TS<->SQL rounding drift in the positive-amount domain. (2) ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses shape guard — dateFields excludes 'paid_at'; numberFields includes 'tax_amount' and 'exchange_rate'; stringFields includes 'currency'. MAIN-LOOP integration verification owed after migration: (a) raw INSERT with currency='EUR', exchange_rate=0.42, amount=1000, amount_base=NULL -> assert amount_base=420.000 (trigger fired); (b) createExpense insert -> assert amount_base equals the service-computed value, unchanged (trigger no-op). No existing expense/import test file exists.
dependencies: EXP-065 — shares ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses (dateFields/uniqueFields). This design already removes the phantom paid_at; EXP-065's config edit must rebase on it so paid_at is removed once.
EXP-066 — shares ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses + ImportWizard export columns; both add money columns to the same map. Coordinate one column definition.

EXP-031/EXP-063/EXP-064 — route-import-through-RPC / feature-gating / created_by stamping. This trigger is the safety net they build on; they are the deferred larger fix and should land after, reusing this trigger rather than recomputing base.
EXP-020 — monetary CHECKs (amount>=0, exchange_rate>0). Complements this trigger; no hard ordering dependency but touches the same columns.

Shared file note for main-loop sequencing: src/lib/expensesService.ts is NOT edited here (trigger is the chokepoint); ENTITY_CONFIGS in src/lib/importExportService.ts IS edited — sequence vs EXP-065/EXP-066.
design.blastRadius: The BEFORE trigger fires on EVERY expenses INSERT/UPDATE, so the blast surface is the entire expense write path — which is why fill-only-when-NULL is the load-bearing guard: createExpense/updateExpense compute and pass amount_base/tax_amount_base non-NULL, making the trigger a verified no-op there (no double conversion, no clobbering of a manually overridden rate). The only rows it changes are those written without base values (imports, raw SQL, legacy/backfill). Trigger ordering is the second risk: it MUST run after set_expenses_tenant_and_audit so NEW.tenant_id is set before _fin_base_currency(NEW.tenant_id); guaranteed by the zz_ name via Postgres alphabetical same-timing ordering. If tenant_id were ever NULL, _fin_base_currency safely falls back to 'USD'->2dp rather than erroring. Reports protected (all read amount_base): getExpenseStats, getExpensesByCategory, financialReportsService P&L/cash-flow/revenue-by-case — once imports carry correct base, their amount_base ?? amount*rate fallbacks stop firing for imports. NOT touched: financial_transactions ledger (append-only; import still posts no ledger row — that gap is EXP-031, deferred), vat_records, invoices/sales VAT (calculateVATForPeriod in vatService.ts is untouched — EXP-032 stays deferred), banking. exchange_rate=0 (EXP-020) would also zero amount_base via the trigger, but that pre-exists and is a separate finding; the trigger does not worsen it. The migration is function+trigger only (no column shape change) but CI still requires database.types.ts regen + manifest entry (schema-drift / migration-manifest gates).

issues: FALSE CENTRAL CLAIM: paid_at is NOT a phantom column. Live schema confirms expenses.paid_at exists (timestamp with time zone, nullable). The design's instruction to set dateFields -> ['expense_date','approved_at'] (REMOVE paid_at) is a regression: it drops the ability to import the paid timestamp and contradicts the design's own EXP-065 dependency note. paid_at should be KEPT.

Trigger is a complete guarantee only for INSERT and NULL-base rows. fill-only-when-NULL means a raw UPDATE that changes amount without nulling amount_base leaves a stale base. The summary's framing ('NO write path can leave them unset') overstates the guarantee; the real win is the import INSERT path + NULL-base legacy rows.

exchange_rate=0 sharp edge: a literal '0' rate in a CSV passes through and the trigger computes amount_base = amount*0 = 0. Deferred to EXP-020 but not guarded here. A blank rate is safer (parseFloat -> NaN -> field skipped -> DB default exchange_rate=1).

Verified-correct (no fix): _fin_base_currency(p_tenant uuid)->text and _fin_currency_decimals(p_code text)->integer exist as SECURITY DEFINER; trigger ordering via zz_ prefix is correct (set_tenant_and_audit_fields sets tenant_id when NULL on INSERT, fires first alphabetically); createExpense computes non-NULL base so trigger is a no-op there; getExpenseStats fallback at lines 671-672 matches exactly; stringFields columns currency/vendor/reference/notes all exist; dropdown union at line 605 is the correct anchor.

ImportWizard empty-value guard (line 210) drops blank currency cells -> falls to DB default 'USD' silently, which is the exact corruption mode for a non-USD-base lab. Advisory is guidance-only, not enforcement; full enforcement correctly deferred to EXP-031/063/064.

Migration is additive/reversible; backfill touches only 2 live rows (0 NULL base, 0 foreign ccy) -> negligible lock risk. RLS/tenant isolation intact. financial_transactions, vat_records, sales/invoice VAT untouched.
mustFixBeforeShip: Do NOT remove paid_at from ENTITY_CONFIGS.expenses.dateFields. paid_at is a real column (verified live). Keep dateFields = ['expense_date','approved_at','paid_at']. Fix the design narrative that calls it phantom and reconcile the EXP-065 dependency note (its premise is wrong).

Invert the test's config-shape guard: it currently asserts dateFields EXCLUDES 'paid_at' — that locks in the regression. Assert dateFields INCLUDES 'paid_at' (plus expense_date, approved_at). Keep the numberFields/stringFields assertions.

Scope the migration comment honestly: change 'no write path can leave them unset' to state the trigger fills on INSERT and for NULL-base rows but does NOT recompute base on an UPDATE that mutates amount while amount_base is already non-NULL — so EXP-031 builders don't over-trust the net on the UPDATE path.
verdict.migrationSafety: SAFE and additive. Verified against live DB: function + trigger only, no shape change. New fn is SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path=public + REVOKE ALL FROM public (matches convention). Trigger-ordering claim CORRECT: set_tenant_and_audit_fields sets NEW.tenant_id when NULL on INSERT and zz_ sorts the new BEFORE-row trigger after it (Postgres fires BEFORE row triggers alphabetically), so _fin_base_currency(NEW.tenant_id) sees a populated tenant_id. Backfill is effectively a no-op: 2 rows total, 0 NULL base, 0 foreign-currency -> negligible lock/cost. RLS/tenant isolation untouched. Reversible (DROP TRIGGER + DROP FUNCTION). Caveat: fill-only-when-NULL is a real safety net only for INSERT and NULL-base rows, not a full UPDATE-path guarantee. Does NOT touch financial_transactions (append-only), vat_records, or sales/invoice VAT — blast radius correctly contained. CI still needs database.types.ts regen + manifest entry per the design.

item.title: Two unreconciled expense ledgers — no single source of truth + no reconciliation test
item.scope: Pick one source of truth (expenses table vs financial_transactions) and add a per-period reconciliation test asserting SUM(approved expenses)==SUM(ledger expense); the edit/delete/periodization desync vectors are already closed on main (approved-edit block, void-reverses-ledger, ledger@expense_date), but the architectural divergence + assertion remain.

design.title: Single source of truth + reconciliation guard for the expense↔ledger relationship
design.summary:
EXP-006's original four sub-gaps have ALREADY been remediated in code I verified on this branch (#310–#317): updateExpense throws on approved/paid/voided (expensesService.ts:231-241); deleteExpense reverses every linked ledger row via reverse_financial_transaction + soft-deletes the VAT row + sets status='voided' (252-357); approveExpense posts the ledger at expense_date not approval date (424-437) behind a pending-only state guard + SoD; purchase-VAT with tax_period is written (439-454, createExpenseVATRecord 753-777). Live DB confirms the supporting invariants exist: uq_financial_transactions_expense_ref (partial unique on reference_type='expense'), expenses_status_chk including 'voided', and reverse_financial_transaction (SECURITY DEFINER, compensating NEGATIVE-amount row with reference_type='reversal', transaction_type copied from the original). financialReportsService P&L/cash-flow/revenue-by-case all read the expenses table and already filter deleted_at (lines 105/252/416/497/584/641) — so the audit's "pick one source of truth = keep reports on the expenses table" is effectively in force, and getTransactionStats/getCashFlowSummary correctly net the negative reversal because it carries transaction_type='expense'.

What EXP-006 STILL lacks is the part its title names explicitly: "no single source of truth + NO RECONCILIATION TEST." There is no function or test that asserts the expenses table and the financial_transactions ledger actually tie out, so the invariant can silently rot on the next change. There is also one real residual divergence: reverse_financial_transaction posts the compensating row at now(), while the original posted at expense_date — when a delete/void crosses a month boundary the per-PERIOD ledger nets wrong (original month over-counts, deletion month under-counts) even though the all-time net is zero; and deleteExpense's reversal/VAT/expense writes are several non-atomic client round-trips that desync on partial failure.

This package delivers the SAFE remaining slice: (1) a SECURITY DEFINER reconciliation function reconcile_expense_ledger() returning per-period and total expense-table-vs-ledger deltas (the executable "single source of truth" contract); (2) a thin read-only service wrapper getExpenseLedgerReconciliation() in expensesService.ts; (3) an atomic void_expense(p_expense_id, p_reason) SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does the reverse-ledger + retire-VAT + soft-delete + status='voided' in one transaction and posts the reversal at the ORIGINAL row's transaction_date (fixing the period residual), with deleteExpense rewired to call it; (4) tests asserting the reconciliation math (including reversal-aware netting) and the void atomicity wrapper. It does NOT touch the shared sales/invoice VAT path (EXP-032 deferred) and does not migrate any reader to the ledger.

files.change: (a) Replace the body of deleteExpense (lines 315-357) so it delegates to the new atomic RPC instead of doing the reverse-loop + vat update + attachment update + expense update as separate client round-trips. New body: call `const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('void_expense', { p_expense_id: id, p_reason: 'Expense deleted' }); if (error) throw error; return data;`. Keep the JSDoc comment block (316-318) updated to say the reverse+VAT-retire+soft-delete now happen atomically server-side. Remove the now-unused inline financial_transactions/vat_records/expense_attachments queries. NOTE: this changes deleteExpense from void to returning the voided expense row — check the two callers (ExpensesList.tsx single-row delete and bulk archive map) tolerate the return (they ignore it today). (b) Add a new exported read-only wrapper after getExpensesByCategory (~line 741): `export const getExpenseLedgerReconciliation = async (filters?: { dateFrom?: string; dateTo?: string }) => { const params: Database['public']['Functions']['reconcile_expense_ledger']['Args'] = {}; if (filters?.dateFrom) params.p_date_from = filters.dateFrom; if (filters?.dateTo) params.p_date_to = filters.dateTo; const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('reconcile_expense_ledger', params); if (error) throw error; return data; };` (c) Add both new functions to the expensesService object export (lines 779-797): getExpenseLedgerReconciliation. (Types come from a regenerated database.types.ts after the migration applies.)

Add two new describe blocks. (1) 'getExpenseLedgerReconciliation' — mock rpc to resolve a sample payload and assert the wrapper calls supabase.rpc('reconcile_expense_ledger', {}) with no params, and with {p_date_from,p_date_to} when filters are passed, and returns data unchanged (mirror the recordExpenseDisbursement test style at lines 82-116). (2) 'deleteExpense (atomic void)' — mock rpc to resolve {data:{id:'e1',status:'voided'},error:null}; assert deleteExpense('e1') calls supabase.rpc('void_expense',{p_expense_id:'e1',p_reason:'Expense deleted'}) and that an rpc error is surfaced (rejects). Reuse the hoisted from/rpc mocks already at the top of the file.

Pure-function unit test of the reconciliation NETTING contract (no DB), so the math is guarded even though the RPC runs server-side. Extract the netting rule into the test as the spec: for a given period, expected ledger expense total = SUM over financial_transactions where transaction_type='expense' AND deleted_at IS NULL of coalesce(amount_base,amount) — which INCLUDES reference_type='reversal' rows (negative) — and must equal SUM over expenses where status IN ('approved','paid') AND deleted_at IS NULL of coalesce(amount_base,amount). Provide a small JS reference implementation `reconcile(expenses, ledger)` in the test file that buckets both sides by YYYY-MM and computes deltas, and assert: (i) a clean approved expense + its single posted ledger row reconcile to delta 0; (ii) a voided expense (excluded from expenses side) whose ledger has original+reversal nets to 0 on BOTH sides; (iii) a posted-but-not-reversed orphan (expense voided, reversal missing) produces a non-zero delta the function flags. This documents and locks the reversal-aware contract the SQL function implements.

design.migrationSql:
-- EXP-006: reconciliation function + atomic void RPC for the expense↔ledger relationship.
-- Both SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path=public; authorize via has_role/tenant fns
-- because RLS is bypassed under definer. No table DDL — purely additive functions.

-- 1) Read-only reconciliation: per-period and total delta between the expenses table
--    (the chosen source of truth) and the financial_transactions expense ledger.
--    Ledger side sums transaction_type='expense' AND deleted_at IS NULL, which already
--    INCLUDES the negative reference_type='reversal' compensating rows, so a voided
--    expense (excluded on the expenses side) nets to 0 on the ledger side too.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.reconcile_expense_ledger(
  p_date_from date DEFAULT NULL,
  p_date_to   date DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  period            text,
  expenses_base     numeric,
  ledger_base       numeric,
  delta_base        numeric
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_tenant uuid;
BEGIN
  v_tenant := get_current_tenant_id();
  IF v_tenant IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'reconcile_expense_ledger: no tenant context'
      USING ERRCODE = 'insufficient_privilege';
  END IF;
  -- Reading financial data is staff-level; gate to non-viewer staff.
  IF NOT is_staff_user() THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'reconcile_expense_ledger: insufficient role'
      USING ERRCODE = 'insufficient_privilege';
  END IF;

  RETURN QUERY
  WITH exp AS (
    SELECT to_char(e.expense_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS period,
           SUM(COALESCE(e.amount_base, e.amount)) AS base
    FROM expenses e
    WHERE e.tenant_id = v_tenant
      AND e.deleted_at IS NULL
      AND e.status IN ('approved','paid')
      AND (p_date_from IS NULL OR e.expense_date >= p_date_from)
      AND (p_date_to   IS NULL OR e.expense_date <= p_date_to)
    GROUP BY 1
  ),
  led AS (
    SELECT to_char(ft.transaction_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS period,
           SUM(COALESCE(ft.amount_base, ft.amount)) AS base
    FROM financial_transactions ft
    WHERE ft.tenant_id = v_tenant
      AND ft.deleted_at IS NULL
      AND ft.transaction_type = 'expense'
      AND (p_date_from IS NULL OR ft.transaction_date >= p_date_from)
      AND (p_date_to   IS NULL OR ft.transaction_date <= p_date_to)
    GROUP BY 1
  )
  SELECT COALESCE(exp.period, led.period) AS period,
         COALESCE(exp.base, 0)            AS expenses_base,
         COALESCE(led.base, 0)            AS ledger_base,
         COALESCE(exp.base, 0) - COALESCE(led.base, 0) AS delta_base
  FROM exp FULL OUTER JOIN led ON exp.period = led.period
  ORDER BY 1;
END;
$$;

REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.reconcile_expense_ledger(date, date) FROM PUBLIC;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.reconcile_expense_ledger(date, date) TO authenticated;

-- 2) Atomic void: reverse every posted ledger row, retire the VAT row, soft-delete
--    attachments + the expense, and flip status to 'voided' in ONE transaction.
--    Fixes the deleteExpense non-atomic client round-trips and the period residual:
--    the reversal is posted at the ORIGINAL ledger row's transaction_date (the
--    expense_date), not now(), so per-period reconciliation nets to 0 in-period.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.void_expense(
  p_expense_id uuid,
  p_reason     text DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS expenses
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_tenant uuid;
  v_uid    uuid;
  v_exp    expenses%ROWTYPE;
  v_ft     financial_transactions%ROWTYPE;
  v_now    timestamptz := now();
BEGIN
  v_tenant := get_current_tenant_id();
  IF v_tenant IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'void_expense: no tenant context' USING ERRCODE = 'insufficient_privilege';
  END IF;
  v_uid := auth.uid();

  -- Removing financial records is the admin-gated authority (matches expenses_delete
  -- intent and EXP-018's recommendation to align soft-delete with the admin gate).
  IF NOT is_tenant_admin() THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'void_expense: only an admin may void/delete an expense'
      USING ERRCODE = 'insufficient_privilege';
  END IF;

  SELECT * INTO v_exp FROM expenses
  WHERE id = p_expense_id AND tenant_id = v_tenant AND deleted_at IS NULL
  FOR UPDATE;
  IF NOT FOUND THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'void_expense: expense % not found', p_expense_id
      USING ERRCODE = 'foreign_key_violation';
  END IF;

  -- Reverse every posted (non-reversal) ledger row for this expense, dating the
  -- compensating entry to the ORIGINAL row's transaction_date for in-period netting.
  IF v_exp.status IN ('approved','paid') THEN
    FOR v_ft IN
      SELECT * FROM financial_transactions
      WHERE tenant_id = v_tenant
        AND reference_type = 'expense'
        AND reference_id = p_expense_id
        AND deleted_at IS NULL
        AND NOT EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM financial_transactions r
          WHERE r.reference_type = 'reversal'
            AND r.reference_id = financial_transactions.id
            AND r.deleted_at IS NULL
        )
    LOOP
      INSERT INTO financial_transactions (
        tenant_id, transaction_type, amount, currency, transaction_date,
        description, category_id, reference_type, reference_id, bank_account_id,
        exchange_rate, rate_source, amount_base, status, notes, created_by
      ) VALUES (
        v_tenant, v_ft.transaction_type, -v_ft.amount, v_ft.currency, v_ft.transaction_date,
        'Reversal of ' || COALESCE(NULLIF(v_ft.description,''), v_ft.id::text)
          || COALESCE(' — ' || NULLIF(p_reason,''), ''),
        v_ft.category_id, 'reversal', v_ft.id, v_ft.bank_account_id,
        v_ft.exchange_rate, v_ft.rate_source,
        -v_ft.amount_base, 'posted', p_reason, v_uid
      );

      IF v_ft.bank_account_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        UPDATE bank_accounts SET current_balance =
          COALESCE(current_balance, 0)
          - CASE WHEN v_ft.transaction_type = 'income' THEN v_ft.amount ELSE -v_ft.amount END
        WHERE id = v_ft.bank_account_id AND tenant_id = v_tenant;
      END IF;
    END LOOP;

    -- Retire the input-VAT row so VAT period totals stop counting voided spend.
    UPDATE vat_records SET deleted_at = v_now
    WHERE tenant_id = v_tenant
      AND record_id = p_expense_id
      AND record_type IN ('purchase','expense')
      AND deleted_at IS NULL;
  END IF;

  UPDATE expense_attachments SET deleted_at = v_now
  WHERE expense_id = p_expense_id AND tenant_id = v_tenant AND deleted_at IS NULL;

  UPDATE expenses SET deleted_at = v_now, status = 'voided'
  WHERE id = p_expense_id AND tenant_id = v_tenant
  RETURNING * INTO v_exp;

  RETURN v_exp;
END;
$$;

REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.void_expense(uuid, text) FROM PUBLIC;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.void_expense(uuid, text) TO authenticated;
design.tests: src/lib/reconcileExpenseLedger.test.ts (new): a pure JS reference `reconcile(expenses, ledger)` that buckets both sides by YYYY-MM with the reversal-aware netting rule, asserting (i) clean approved expense + posted row → delta 0; (ii) voided expense excluded on the expenses side + original/reversal on the ledger side → delta 0 in-period; (iii) orphan posted row with no reversal → non-zero delta flagged. This locks the exact contract reconcile_expense_ledger() SQL implements. src/lib/expensesService.test.ts (edit): add 'getExpenseLedgerReconciliation' block asserting the rpc name + params (empty and date-filtered) and pass-through return, mirroring the recordExpenseDisbursement tests (lines 82-116); add 'deleteExpense (atomic void)' block asserting deleteExpense calls rpc('void_expense',{p_expense_id,p_reason:'Expense deleted'}) and surfaces rpc errors. All use the existing hoisted from/rpc mocks. Manual/live verification (main loop, post-apply): run `select * from reconcile_expense_ledger();` and confirm every delta_base is 0 on current data; void an approved expense via the RPC and re-run to confirm it stays 0 in the original period.
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts with EXP-005/EXP-017/EXP-014/EXP-019/EXP-016 — already merged on this branch. The main loop must sequence: edits to expensesService.ts (deleteExpense rewrite + new wrapper + export) must land after any other in-flight expensesService.ts item to avoid conflicting hunks.

Depends on reverse_financial_transaction (live), the uq_financial_transactions_expense_ref partial unique index (live, EXP-001), and expenses_status_chk including 'voided' (live, EXP-009/020) — all verified present.

Migration must be applied via MCP by the MAIN LOOP (not a subagent); database.types.ts must be regenerated via mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types AFTER apply so the new Functions.reconcile_expense_ledger / void_expense Args types exist before the service edits typecheck.

EXP-032 (VAT period bucketing) is explicitly DEFERRED here — see blastRadius. EXP-026 (financialReportsService deleted_at) is already fixed in code; this item assumes that and is a no-op if it regresses.
design.blastRadius:
VAT (EXP-032 deferred — SAFE slice boundary): I deliberately do NOT touch calculateVATForPeriod/getQuarterlyVATSummary in vatService.ts. Those are SHARED with invoices/sales and the audit flags that sales vat_records may lack tax_period; changing the period-keying there risks mis-bucketing every sale's output VAT. The expense side already writes tax_period from expense_date (createExpenseVATRecord), and void_expense soft-deletes the VAT row, so expense input-VAT is internally consistent without re-keying the shared reader. Deferred: migrating calculateVATForPeriod from created_at to tax_period/economic-date for BOTH sales and purchases — needs its own item with a sales-side tax_period backfill and a regression test on the invoice VAT-return path.

Ledger/Transactions module: void_expense replicates reverse_financial_transaction's exact compensating-row + bank-balance logic, except it (a) dates the reversal at the original's transaction_date instead of now() and (b) skips rows already reversed. Because the reversal still carries transaction_type='expense' with a negative amount, getTransactionStats/getCashFlowSummary/getMonthlyCashFlow continue to net correctly — now in the correct period. The append-only prevent_financial_transactions_mutation trigger is untouched (we only INSERT). reverse_financial_transaction itself is left in place and still callable elsewhere; void_expense does not call it (to control the date), avoiding double-reversal via the NOT EXISTS guard.

P&L / cash-flow / case-profitability / reports: read the expenses table and already filter deleted_at; void_expense sets deleted_at + status='voided', so they drop voided spend exactly as today. No reader is migrated to the ledger — the source-of-truth decision (expenses table) is preserved, and the new reconcile_expense_ledger() is read-only diagnostics, never wired into a report total.

Banking/invoices: void_expense touches bank_accounts.current_balance only for ledger rows that carry a bank_account_id (i.e. paid expenses with a disbursement), reversing the original debit — same effect the existing reverse RPC has; invoices are never read or written. Permissions: both new fns authorize inside the definer (is_staff_user for read, is_tenant_admin for void) so RLS bypass is safe; void_expense's admin gate also closes EXP-018's soft-delete-too-broad gap for the RPC path (the raw UPDATE path in any remaining caller is separate and out of scope here).

issues: CRITICAL money corruption: void_expense does NOT reverse a PAID expense's cash side. record_expense_disbursement records the disbursement as a bank_transactions row + payment_disbursements row and debits bank_accounts.current_balance/current_balance_base DIRECTLY; the financial_transactions accrual row posted at approval has bank_account_id = NULL. void_expense's bank-balance reversal is gated on `IF v_ft.bank_account_id IS NOT NULL`, so for a paid expense it credits nothing back, and it never touches the bank_transactions row, the completed payment_disbursements row, or current_balance_base. Voiding a paid expense leaves the bank permanently over-debited and an orphan 'completed' disbursement. The design's blastRadius claim that void_expense 'reverses the original debit ... same effect the existing reverse RPC has' is FALSE for paid expenses.

HIGH: void_expense hand-rolls a negative-amount INSERT into financial_transactions instead of calling reverse_financial_transaction, purely to back-date the reversal to the original transaction_date. This (a) violates the stated hard constraint 'reverse only via reverse_financial_transaction', (b) duplicates the exact compensating-row + bank-balance logic that will drift from the canonical RPC, and (c) back-dates a financial mutation into a possibly-closed prior accounting/VAT period. The shipped reverse_financial_transaction deliberately posts at now() for exactly this reason; the design treats that as a bug without weighing closed-period correctness.

MEDIUM: createFinancialTransaction (approveExpense) posts the accrual ledger row with NO bank_account_id, so the design's whole premise that voiding nets the ledger AND the bank balance is only ever true for the (non-existent) case of a directly-bank-linked expense ledger row. The cash side lives entirely outside financial_transactions, which the reconcile_expense_ledger function also never checks — so reconciliation will report delta=0 while the bank is actually wrong.

MEDIUM: vat_records has no unique constraint on record_id and record_type='purchase' is the SHARED sales/purchase VAT type; void_expense deletes by (record_id, record_type IN ('purchase','expense')). Safe today only because expense record_ids are expense PKs, but it is an unguarded shared-namespace delete on the VAT table that EXP-032 is explicitly deferring — fragile coupling.

LOW: deleteExpense changes signature from void to returning the voided expenses row; design flags but does not verify the ExpensesList.tsx single-delete and bulk-archive callers tolerate it. Also void_expense's admin-only gate (is_tenant_admin) is STRICTER than the current deleteExpense path (no role gate) and stricter than record_expense_disbursement's has_role('accounts') — a silent permission tightening that may break existing manager/accounts delete flows.

NOTE (safe part): the reconcile_expense_ledger function and getExpenseLedgerReconciliation wrapper are genuinely net-new, additive, read-only, correctly authorized inside the definer (is_staff_user verified live), and tenant-scoped. The pure-function reconciliation test is sound. This slice could ship on its own.
mustFixBeforeShip: Make void_expense reverse the PAID cash side: detect the expense's payment_disbursements/bank_transactions, credit bank_accounts.current_balance AND current_balance_base back, and soft-void the disbursement + write the compensating bank_transactions row. As written it silently corrupts bank balances for any paid expense. (Or: restrict void_expense to approved-not-paid and require disbursement reversal first.)

Stop hand-rolling the negative financial_transactions INSERT. Either call reverse_financial_transaction (accepting now()-dated reversal) or get an explicit, separately-reviewed decision to back-date reversals — including how closed-period VAT/reporting totals are protected. Back-dating into a prior period is a correctness/compliance change, not a 'residual fix'.

Decide whether reconcile_expense_ledger must also tie out the cash/bank side (disbursements vs bank_transactions), since the accrual ledger carries no bank_account_id and the real cash movement is invisible to the current reconciliation query — otherwise it reports balanced books over a corrupted bank balance.

Verify ExpensesList.tsx single-delete and bulk-archive callers tolerate deleteExpense's new return value, and reconcile the void_expense is_tenant_admin gate against the existing (ungated) delete and accounts-level disbursement permission to avoid silently revoking manager/accounts delete access.
Split the safe reconciliation function + wrapper + pure-function test into a standalone ship; hold the void_expense rewrite until the cash-side and back-dating issues are resolved.

item.title: No DB-side state-machine trigger backstop on expense transitions
item.scope: Service now guards transitions (approve/reject require pending, approved/paid edits blocked), but there is no DB trigger enforcing the allowed-transition map — direct API/SQL writes can still desync status; add a transition-guard trigger.

design.title: DB-side state-machine trigger backstop on expense status transitions

files.change: New migration = the migrationSql verbatim. MAIN LOOP applies via mcp__supabase__apply_migration (not a subagent). zz_ prefix orders the trigger after set_expenses_tenant_and_audit so it validates the final NEW row. WHEN clause limits firing to status changes / soft-deletes. No status CHECK change; no new columns.

Append one table row: | <timestamp> | enforce_expense_status_transition_trigger | Function + Trigger | EXP-009 DB-side state-machine backstop: BEFORE UPDATE trigger validating OLD.status->NEW.status (draft/rejected->pending, pending->approved, pending->rejected, approved->paid; ->voided only via soft-delete; voided terminal), RAISEing check_violation otherwise. Mirrors shipped service guards so no path breaks; stops a direct table UPDATE (RLS only checks has_role('accounts')) from illegal moves e.g. draft->paid or approved->rejected (dangling ledger). Live data 1 draft + 1 pending so non-breaking. No public-schema column change -> no database.types regen expected. | expense-hardening |. Required by the migration-manifest CI gate.

MAIN LOOP runs mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types AFTER applying the migration and saves it. An arg-less trigger function normally yields NO diff in generated Tables/Functions types, so the schema-drift gate should pass unchanged; regenerate to confirm zero drift and commit only if a diff is emitted.

OPTIONAL comment-only: one-line note above approveExpense/rejectExpense/markExpenseAsPaid that DB trigger enforce_expense_status_transition is the backstop (EXP-009). No logic change - the service guards already enforce the same map and remain the primary friendly-error path. SHARED with EXP-005/016/017; sequence this touch last or drop it to avoid conflicts.

design.tests: Extend src/lib/expensesService.test.ts (already mocks supabaseClient with a chainable thenable builder) and add a rolled-back live-DB check run by the MAIN LOOP. 1) New describe('state-machine transition guards'), table-driven, mocking the initial-fetch .maybeSingle() to return OLD status: approveExpense rejects when status!=='pending'; approveExpense rejects when created_by===approvedBy; rejectExpense rejects when status!=='pending'; markExpenseAsPaid rejects when status!=='approved'; updateExpense rejects when current status in {approved,paid,voided}; submitExpense calls builder.in('status',['draft','rejected']) and the update payload has no created_by key (locks the EXP-009 authorship-overwrite regression). 2) Live DB trigger verification (mcp__supabase__execute_sql in BEGIN; ... ROLLBACK; under accounts context): draft->paid expect check_violation; draft->pending ok; pending->approved ok; approved->rejected expect check_violation (dangling-ledger case); approved->paid ok; paid->rejected expect check_violation; SET deleted_at=now(),status='voided' from any state ok (mirrors deleteExpense); voided->pending expect check_violation (terminal); status-unchanged field edit (SET vendor='x' on a pending row) ok (WHEN clause skips trigger); confirm record_expense_disbursement still completes its internal approved->paid UPDATE.
design.blastRadius: Why it does not break adjacent systems: (1) record_expense_disbursement (banking) does approved->paid by direct UPDATE - explicitly allowed; verified against its live definition. (2) deleteExpense sets status='voided' + deleted_at from ANY state - the soft-delete branch allows this, so archiving approved/paid/draft expenses and the in-function ledger reversal (reverse_financial_transaction) are untouched. (3) The existing BEFORE trigger set_expenses_tenant_and_audit is unaffected; the new trigger is named zz_ so it runs after it and validates the final row. (4) Field-only edits do not change status, so the WHEN clause skips the trigger entirely - zero overhead/behaviour change on the edit hot path. (5) Imports/CSV (importExportService, ImportWizard) insert at DEFAULT 'draft' and never UPDATE status -> unaffected. Out of scope and safe: invoices/quotes/payments/VAT/reports/banking balances - trigger is scoped to public.expenses only; financial_transactions stays append-only, reversed only via the existing RPC inside deleteExpense (unchanged). Residual risk (why medium): any undiscovered legitimate status UPDATE outside the five mapped edges would now be rejected. Mitigated by grepping .update on 'expenses' with a status key (only the service mutators + record_expense_disbursement exist) and by the rolled-back live verification before merge. The map is intentionally minimal; a future legitimate edge (e.g. paid->refunded) is a one-line additive change.
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts with EXP-005 (field capture), EXP-016 (resubmit), EXP-017 (disbursement, shipped). The EXP-009 touch to this file is comment-only/optional - sequence it last or drop it to avoid conflicts.

Depends on shipped migrations 20260621212017 (status CHECK includes 'voided', DEFAULT 'draft') and 20260622074117 (record_expense_disbursement does approved->paid). The map's approved->paid and ->voided-via-soft-delete edges keep both compatible - verified against live source.
Orthogonal to EXP-008/EXP-011/EXP-012 (SoD/RLS/approver-set): enforces the state machine only and remains the innermost backstop if those add actor/status predicates or a transition RPC.

issues: Trigger ordering verified safe
Edge map matches live service
Only record_expense_disbursement updates status, allowed
Not duplicate, DB backstop is the real gap
Minor: illegal moves surface raw Postgres error
No blast radius into VAT or banking
mustFixBeforeShip: Document rollback drop trigger and function
Run rolled-back live verification before merge
Regenerate types and confirm zero drift
verdict.migrationSafety: Additive and safe. New trigger sorts after the audit trigger and validates the final row. Status CHECK already has all six states. Live data 1 draft 1 pending. record_expense_disbursement approved to paid is allowed. Idempotent migration, brief lock on 2 rows, no column change so zero types drift. RLS untouched. No rollback documented - add it.

item.title: rejectExpense overwrites notes with rejection reason; no dedicated rejection_reason column
item.scope: Add rejection_reason/rejected_by/rejected_at columns, write reason there leaving notes intact, render the dedicated field in the list rejected-row preview.

design.title: rejectExpense — verify dedicated rejection_reason path is intact and add regression-guard tests (fix already shipped)
design.summary: EXP-007 (audit §3.2/§3.3) reported that rejectExpense did `notes = reason`, destroying the submitter's notes, and reused approved_by/approved_at to record the rejector, with no dedicated columns. Grounding against the ACTUAL current code (post #310-#317): this is ALREADY FIXED end-to-end. Live `expenses` schema now has `rejection_reason text`, `rejected_by uuid`, `rejected_at timestamptz` (plus `notes text` still present and untouched) — verified via information_schema. `src/lib/expensesService.ts:459-492` `rejectExpense` writes status='rejected', rejection_reason=reason, rejected_by=rejectedBy, rejected_at=now() to those dedicated columns, NEVER touches `notes`, and guards `status !== 'pending'` (closing the §3.3 "reject from any status" edge-case that would leave an approved expense's ledger dangling). `updateExpense` (lines 245-251) and `submitExpense` (lines 359-380) clear rejection_reason/rejected_by/rejected_at on resubmit (EXP-016). EXPENSE_LIST_COLUMNS includes `rejection_reason` (line 93); `ExpensesList.tsx:667-669` renders `expense.rejection_reason` (not `notes`); `ExpenseDetailModal.tsx:97-107` shows `notes` and `rejection_reason` as two separate fields. database.types.ts already carries all three columns on the expenses Row/Insert/Update (lines ~6154-6228). No `notes = reason` / `notes: reason` write survives anywhere in src/. So no schema migration, no service change, no UI change is required. The ONE real gap: there is zero automated regression guard — expensesService.test.ts covers getExpensesByCategory, EXPENSE_LIST_COLUMNS and recordExpenseDisbursement, but nothing asserts rejectExpense preserves notes, writes the dedicated columns, or rejects a non-pending expense; ExpenseDetailModal.test.tsx never asserts the rejection_reason field renders. Safe scope = lock the fix in with tests so a future refactor cannot silently reintroduce the destructive overwrite.

files.change: Add a new `describe('rejectExpense (EXP-007 — reason goes to its own column, notes preserved)')` block after the existing recordExpenseDisbursement block (after line 116). The existing makeQuery helper is for SELECT-style chains; rejectExpense does TWO calls on supabase.from('expenses'): (1) .select('status').eq('id',id).maybeSingle() to read current status, then (2) .update({...}).eq('id',id).eq('status','pending').select().maybeSingle(). Build a small inline mock in each test: from.mockReturnValueOnce(<status-read builder>) then from.mockReturnValueOnce(<update builder>). Status-read builder: { select:vi.fn(()=>b), eq:vi.fn(()=>b), maybeSingle:vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({data:{status:'pending'},error:null}) }. Update builder: capture the payload — { update:vi.fn((p)=>{captured=p;return b;}), eq:vi.fn(()=>b), select:vi.fn(()=>b), maybeSingle:vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({data:{id:'e1',status:'rejected',notes:'Reimburse via payroll, see receipt #44',rejection_reason:'Missing VAT receipt'},error:null}) }. Import rejectExpense (and approveExpense if you also want the SoD/collision sibling assertions) by extending the existing import on line 15. Tests to add: (1) 'writes the reason to rejection_reason and never to notes' — call rejectExpense('e1','mgr-9','Missing VAT receipt'); assert captured has rejection_reason==='Missing VAT receipt', rejected_by==='mgr-9', status==='rejected', and that captured does NOT contain a `notes` key (expect(captured).not.toHaveProperty('notes')) and does NOT contain `approved_by`/`approved_at` (the §3.2 secondary collision); assert rejected_at is a string. (2) 'stamps the rejector in rejected_by, not approved_by' — assert captured.rejected_by set, captured.approved_by undefined. (3) 'refuses to reject a non-pending expense (closes the EXP-007 §3.3 edge-case)' — make the status-read builder return {data:{status:'approved'}}; expect rejectExpense(...) to reject with /Only a pending expense can be rejected/ and assert the update builder's update mock was NOT called (from called once). (4) 'throws when the expense does not exist' — status-read returns {data:null}; expect rejection /not found/. Reset from/rpc are already handled by the top-level beforeEach.

Add a test asserting the EXP-007 contract in the detail view: render ExpenseDetailModal with an expense fixture carrying BOTH notes:'Reimburse via payroll, see receipt #44' and rejection_reason:'Missing VAT receipt' and status:'rejected'. Assert screen.getByText('Reimburse via payroll, see receipt #44') (the Notes field, preserved) AND screen.getByText('Missing VAT receipt') (the Rejection reason field) both appear — proving the submitter's notes survive rejection and the reason is shown as a distinct field (ExpenseDetailModal.tsx:97-107). Reuse the existing fixture/render harness in this file (it already sets notes:'urgent restock' on line 32) — add rejection_reason + status to a rejected variant.

design.tests: In src/lib/expensesService.test.ts add `describe('rejectExpense (EXP-007)')` with 4 cases: (1) reason is written to rejection_reason and the update payload has NO `notes` key and NO approved_by/approved_at (asserts both the primary notes-clobber and the secondary approved_by-collision are gone); (2) rejector is stamped in rejected_by not approved_by, rejected_at is set; (3) rejecting a non-pending (e.g. 'approved') expense throws /Only a pending expense can be rejected/ and never issues the UPDATE (asserts the §3.3 state-guard); (4) missing expense throws /not found/. In src/components/financial/ExpenseDetailModal.test.tsx add a case rendering a rejected expense with both notes and rejection_reason and asserting BOTH strings render as separate fields (notes preserved + reason shown). These are pure regression guards — no production code changes — so they must pass immediately against the current tree (run: npx vitest run src/lib/expensesService.test.ts src/components/financial/ExpenseDetailModal.test.tsx). If any fails, the fix has regressed and the failure is the signal.
design.blastRadius: None to production. This package adds ONLY test files; it changes no service, migration, type, or UI code, so invoices/VAT/banking/reports/case-finance are untouched. The investigation independently re-confirmed the surrounding money paths are unaffected: rejectExpense never posts to financial_transactions or vat_records (only approveExpense does, at expense_date), so EXP-007's domain has no ledger/VAT exposure. The state-guard test (case 3) documents that a rejection cannot fire on an already-approved expense, which is exactly what protects the append-only ledger from a dangling reversal. Because no schema or types change, schema-drift/typecheck/tenant-table CI gates are unaffected. The only failure mode is a test that fails against current code — which would mean the shipped fix regressed, i.e. the test is doing its job.
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts (read-only here) and src/pages/financial/ExpensesList.tsx with EXP-005/EXP-016/EXP-019/EXP-010 etc. — but this item edits NEITHER, only the two *.test files, so it can land in any order with zero merge contention against other expense items.

Logically depends on migration 20260621212017 + PRs #310-#317 (already shipped/merged) which added the rejection_reason/rejected_by/rejected_at columns and the dedicated-column write path — this item just locks that in. No new ordering constraint it imposes on others.

EXP-010 (force-stamp approved_by/rejected_by via DB trigger using auth.uid()) is the natural follow-up: it would harden the rejected_by this test asserts is set client-side today. If EXP-010 lands first, the rejectExpense test's rejected_by assertion still holds (service still passes rejectedBy; trigger would override server-side, invisible to the mocked client test).

verdict.migrationSafety: No migration. needsMigration:false is correct. The rejection_reason/rejected_by/rejected_at columns already exist live (added by migration 20260621212017 + PRs #310-#317, both verified merged into main per the audit memory and the at-rest code). This package adds ONLY two *.test.ts(x) files — zero schema, types, RLS, tenant-isolation, or production-code change. No lock risk, nothing to reverse. schema-drift/typecheck/tenant-table CI gates are untouched.
issues: Verified: rejectExpense (expensesService.ts:459-492) is exactly as the design claims — writes status/rejection_reason/rejected_by/rejected_at to dedicated columns, never touches notes, never reuses approved_by, and guards status!=='pending' before the UPDATE with a second .eq('id').eq('status','pending') guard on the write. The §3.2 (approved_by collision) and §3.3 (reject-from-any-status) bugs are both genuinely closed. This item does NOT duplicate shipped CODE — it only adds the missing regression guard, which is legitimate.

Verified: updateExpense (245-251) and submitExpense (359-380) both clear rejection_reason/rejected_by/rejected_at on resubmit (EXP-016). ExpenseDetailModal.tsx:97-107 renders notes and rejection_reason as two separate Fields. Design anchors are accurate.

MOCK CORRECTNESS RISK (test will pass against current code, but is brittle): rejectExpense issues TWO .eq() calls on the update chain (.update().eq('id').eq('status','pending').select().maybeSingle()). The design's update builder uses eq:vi.fn(()=>b) which returns the same builder on every eq call, so the double-.eq() chain resolves correctly. Good. But the status-read and update builders must be wired with from.mockReturnValueOnce in the exact call order (read first, then update) — if a future refactor adds a third supabase.from('expenses') call (e.g. an audit/custody write on reject), the second mockReturnValueOnce would be consumed by the wrong call and the test would break spuriously. Acceptable for a regression guard but worth a code comment in the test.

Assertion `expect(captured).not.toHaveProperty('notes')` is correct against current code AND is the right guard — it directly fails if anyone reintroduces notes=reason. Strong test. The approved_by/approved_at not-present assertion is also valid (current update payload omits them).

MINOR GAP the design under-covers: the ExpenseDetailModal test proves the modal RENDERS rejection_reason when the field is present on the fixture — but it does NOT prove the list query (EXPENSE_LIST_COLUMNS) actually SELECTS rejection_reason so the modal receives it. The design notes EXPENSE_LIST_COLUMNS:93 includes it, but the existing column test (expensesService.test.ts:72) does not assert 'rejection_reason'. Recommend adding 'rejection_reason' to that existing column-list assertion array so the data-path (list selects it -> modal shows it) is fully guarded, not just the render half.

No financial blast radius: confirmed rejectExpense never calls createFinancialTransaction or createExpenseVATRecord (only approveExpense does, at expense_date — lines 426-454). No ledger/VAT/banking/invoice/sales exposure. EXP-032's shared calculateVATForPeriod path is untouched. Test files import nothing from vatService/financialReportsService/caseFinanceService.

item.title: Zero audit trail for expense lifecycle; actor fields client-spoofable
item.scope: Extend DB actor-stamping to expenses forcing created_by:=auth.uid() on insert and approved_by:=auth.uid() on transition (not COALESCE), add updated_by, add append-only expense history (or fold into audit_trails), log case_job_history on case-linked approve/paid.

design.title: Server-forced actor stamping plus audit trail for the expense lifecycle
design.summary: After 310-317 the expenses table has only set_expenses_tenant_and_audit and no actor stamping so created_by is client supplied and spoofable and there is no log_audit_trail or log_case_history in the expense files. See files for the full design.

files.change: Confirmed live: expenses carries only set_expenses_tenant_and_audit running set_tenant_and_audit_fields which stamps tenant_id and timestamps, never an actor column. The v1.2.0 set_audit_actor_fields was never attached. created_by is taken from the client at ExpensesList line 208, approved_by and rejected_by from profile id at lines 231 and 243, so a direct insert can spoof the actor. Grep shows zero log_audit_trail or log_case_history calls in the expense files. log_audit_trail is SECURITY DEFINER and stamps performed_by and tenant server-side; the wrapper logAuditTrail and log_case_history already exist.

files.path: MIGRATION expense_actor_stamping (LAYER A, applied by MAIN LOOP)
files.change: New SECURITY DEFINER trigger fn set_expense_actor_fields before insert and update on expenses, ordered after set_expenses_tenant_and_audit so tenant_id resolves first. INSERT forces created_by to auth uid, keeping a supplied value only when no auth context exists for system or import jobs. UPDATE sets updated_by to auth uid, pins created_by to OLD value so authorship is immutable, and stamps submitted_by approved_by or rejected_by to auth uid on entry into that status. Forcing rather than COALESCE makes the spoof impossible. Exact SQL in the migrationSql field. No DROP or destructive ops.

Import logAuditTrail from auditTrailService and add a helper calling the log_case_history rpc. createExpense after insert at line 216 logs created with status amount category_id. updateExpense widens the pre image select at line 233 to also fetch amount category_id status and logs updated with changed_fields equal to the changed keys plus old pre image before the return at line 312. submitExpense logs submitted. approveExpense logs approved before the createFinancialTransaction call and when case_id is set also calls log_case_history expense_approved. rejectExpense logs rejected with rejection_reason. markExpenseAsPaid logs paid and case-linked also log_case_history expense_paid. deleteExpense after the final update at line 351 logs deleted with new status voided. recordExpenseDisbursement logs disbursed.

The create update and approve audit calls throw for fail fast parity with the ledger so books and trail stay consistent. The delete markPaid and disbursement audit calls are wrapped in try with logger error so a logging failure never blocks the soft-delete or the payment. The existing created_by and approvedBy params are kept for signature stability but are now advisory because the DB trigger forces the real value.
Regenerate via the supabase MCP after the migration. Trigger only change so likely zero table row type diff, but regenerate and commit per migration discipline or confirm a zero diff.

design.tests: New src/lib/__tests__/expensesService.audit.test.ts (Vitest, mock supabaseClient and auditTrailService): assert each mutator emits the right log_audit_trail action and old vs new, approve and markPaid emit log_case_history only when case linked, and a thrown logAuditTrail still lets deleteExpense soft-delete to voided. Plus a DB manual checklist for MAIN LOOP and the usual typecheck and schema-drift regression. Detail in files.
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts with EXP-005 017 019 027 006 014 016 so MAIN LOOP sequences edits; this only adds audit calls and widens one select
Migration applies before the database.types.ts regen
Independent of EXP-027 032 VAT and EXP-006 ledger
Complementary to a future EXP-011 008 transition RPC
design.blastRadius: Trigger fires on every expenses write but touches only actor columns, never amount currency status or tenant, and never updated_at, so money fields, FX snapshots, the EXP-006 void path and the EXP-019 optimistic lock are unaffected. Invoices VAT banking and reports are untouched; the only new writes are audit_trails and case_job_history via existing SECURITY DEFINER RPCs that gate on is_staff_user and stamp tenant server-side, so no cross-tenant leak, and the SoD self-approval block still holds.

verdict.migrationSafety: Migration is additive and safe on the live DB. It only CREATEs set_expense_actor_fields (SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path=public, correct) and a BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE trigger; no DROP of data, no column/table drops. The trigger writes ONLY actor columns (created_by/updated_by/submitted_by/approved_by/rejected_by) — verified all five exist and are nullable on expenses — never amount/currency/status/tenant_id/updated_at, so EXP-006 void, EXP-019 optimistic lock (uses updated_at, untouched), FX snapshots, invoices, sales VAT, banking and reports are all unaffected. Reversible: DROP TRIGGER + DROP FUNCTION. Lock: CREATE TRIGGER takes a momentary ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on expenses, trivial at current volume (1 row). RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation intact; no cross-tenant path since log_audit_trail/log_case_history stamp tenant via get_current_tenant_id() server-side (both verified SECURITY DEFINER). Not duplicate work: confirmed live that only set_expenses_tenant_and_audit exists and set_tenant_and_audit_fields never sets any actor column; the v1.2.0 set_audit_actor_fields was never attached to expenses. One non-fatal nit: the 'order after tenant trigger so tenant_id resolves first' rationale is wrong — the actor fn reads only auth.uid()/status, not tenant_id, so the zset naming is harmless but the justification is incorrect.

The existing logAuditTrail wrapper (src/lib/auditTrailService.ts) takes only 5 args and does NOT pass p_changed_fields. The design's updateExpense 'logs updated with changed_fields' cannot use the wrapper as-is; the wrapper must be extended or a new call added.

approveExpense error policy is backwards: design makes the audit call throw 'for parity with the ledger', but if the audit call is placed before createFinancialTransaction and throws, the expense is already committed as approved (separate non-transactional calls) with NO ledger row — the exact inconsistency the ledger fail-fast prevents. Approve audit logging must be best-effort, not throw.

INSERT only forces created_by when auth.uid() IS NOT NULL; a NULL-auth path (service-role/edge fn) can still supply/spoof created_by, and legitimate import jobs (known issue, mem 8738) running under a real auth context cannot attribute to the real submitter. Interaction unaddressed.

submitExpense already sets submitted_by from a client param and updateExpense's resubmit path sets status='pending'; the new trigger will now overwrite submitted_by with auth.uid() on every pending transition — a behavior change (mostly desirable) not called out in the design.
Trigger-ordering rationale is incorrect: actor fn does not depend on tenant_id resolution; ordering is irrelevant (harmless but indicates incomplete reasoning).
mustFixBeforeShip: Resolve the log_audit_trail overload ambiguity: call the specific signature explicitly (provide all 6 named args incl. p_changed_fields, or disambiguate) and confirm no PGRST203; verify the typed RPC signature in database.types.ts after regen.
Extend logAuditTrail wrapper (or add a call) to actually pass p_changed_fields, since updateExpense's design depends on it.

Make the approveExpense audit/log_case_history calls best-effort (try/catch + logger), NOT fail-fast — throwing there leaves an approved expense with no ledger row. Only create/update may throw without breaking ledger consistency.
Document/handle the submitted_by overwrite on the updateExpense status->pending resubmit path so EXP-016 resubmit behavior is intentional.

item.title: updateExpense/transitions editable on any expense by accounts-or-higher; RLS lacks ownership/status predicate
item.scope: Add created_by-aware + status-aware authorization (submitter edits own while draft/pending; approver role AND created_by<>auth.uid() to approve) enforced in RLS or a SECURITY DEFINER transition RPC, not just service guards.

design.title: created_by and status-aware authorization for expense edit and transitions
design.summary: EXP-011 has two faces (audit 3.3 edit/transition authority; 3.7 ownership/visibility). LIVE DB confirms: expenses_update USING and CHECK both equal (SELECT has_role accounts) with NO status and NO created_by predicate; expenses_select equals USING(true) so every role including viewer reads all rows; expenses_delete equals has_role admin but governs only hard DELETE which the app never issues (dead path; soft-delete rides the UPDATE policy). RESTRICTIVE expenses_tenant_isolation is intact, so this is intra-tenant only. profiles.id IS the auth user id (no user_id column; handle_new_user sets profiles.id to auth.uid), and the frontend stamps created_by from profile.id, so created_by equals auth.uid is a valid RLS ownership predicate. Service updateExpense already blocks approved/paid/voided edits (EXP-006 shipped) but has no ownership check; approveExpense enforces pending-only plus segregation of duties (approver not equal creator, EXP-008); submit/reject/markPaid carry status guards. Fix is defense-in-depth: (1) MIGRATION tightens SELECT to is_staff_user plus submitter-or-approver scope and splits UPDATE into an approver policy keeping owner/admin/manager/accounts transition power plus a submitter policy (created_by equals auth.uid AND status in draft/pending/rejected). (2) SERVICE adds an explicit ownership guard in updateExpense mirroring the status guard so the API path cannot bypass the UI. (3) UI replaces the hardcoded isAccountsRole equals admin-or-accounts with a role-set constant matching RLS (resolving the EXP-012 divergence) and shows Edit only on the submitter own draft/pending/rejected rows. The mine/all toggle UI is deferred.

The migration is additive (DROP IF EXISTS plus CREATE), reversible, and needs no backfill.

issues: Live DB confirms the design's policy baseline exactly (expenses_select USING(true); expenses_update USING+CHECK = has_role('accounts'); expenses_delete = has_role('admin') DELETE-only dead path; RESTRICTIVE tenant_isolation intact). created_by is nullable, 0 nulls, 2 rows, 0 approved/paid -> no backfill needed. These claims hold.

BLAST RADIUS / shared path (not resolved): caseFinanceService.ts queries expenses directly (lines 53, 181, 190) for case profitability and getCaseExpenses. Under the tightened SELECT (is_staff_user AND created_by=auth.uid OR has_role('manager'/'accounts')), a technician who did not create an expense reads 0 rows -> silently understated case expense totals and case margin for the lab's core operator role. The design only flags this to 'VERIFY before applying' and does NOT resolve it.

RLS self-approval backstop is WEAKENED, not strengthened. The new expenses_update_owner has WITH CHECK (created_by = auth.uid()) with NO status constraint. Because the owner and approver policies OR together, a creator can issue a raw UPDATE setting status='approved' on their own pending row -> the RLS no longer blocks creator self-approval (SoD now lives ONLY in approveExpense service code). The old single policy required has_role('accounts') on CHECK for every UPDATE, which did backstop this.

Redundant/misleading predicate: has_role('accounts') already resolves to owner/admin/manager/accounts (verified function def), so 'has_role('manager') OR has_role('accounts')' in the SELECT scope is fully redundant. Harmless but the design mischaracterizes its own scope.

Transitions are safe: approveExpense/rejectExpense/markExpenseAsPaid and record_expense_disbursement all authorize via has_role('accounts') server-side (verified RPC def), and the approver UPDATE policy preserves full has_role('accounts') power, so the UPDATE split does not break the state machine or the disbursement RPC.

Service ownership guard design is sound and back-compatible (actingUserId optional, skips guard when omitted); UI ExpenseRow already carries created_by via EXPENSE_LIST_COLUMNS so the Edit-visibility gate has the data it needs.
Does not duplicate shipped work: EXP-006 status guard and EXP-019 optimistic lock are preserved and untouched; this adds an orthogonal ownership layer.
Session cost flagged CRITICAL at $581.79 by the cost hook -- operator should be aware before further DB introspection.
mustFixBeforeShip: Resolve technician case-expense visibility before tightening SELECT: either add `OR has_role('technician')` to the expenses_select scope, OR (preferred) expose case expense totals via a SECURITY DEFINER aggregate so caseFinanceService.getCaseFinancials/getCaseExpenses do not lose rows for technicians. Without this, case profitability (EXP-014, shipped) silently under-reports for the lab's primary operator role.

Tighten expenses_update_owner WITH CHECK to forbid self-transition into approver-only states: add `AND status IN ('draft','pending','rejected')` to the WITH CHECK (not just USING), so a creator cannot raw-UPDATE their own row to status='approved'/'paid'. Keep SoD in approveExpense as defense-in-depth, but the RLS must not regress the creator-self-approval backstop the current single policy provides.
Drop the redundant has_role('manager') term in the SELECT scope (subsumed by has_role('accounts')) or correct the design narrative to reflect that has_role('accounts') = owner/admin/manager/accounts.
verdict.migrationSafety: Migration is additive and reversible in shape (DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE), touches only expenses_select and expenses_update, leaves RESTRICTIVE expenses_tenant_isolation and expenses_insert intact, needs no backfill (2 rows, 0 null created_by, 0 approved/paid). Lock risk is negligible (policy DDL on a 2-row table; brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on expenses only). BUT it is NOT behavior-safe as written: (1) the SELECT tightening is a live read regression for technicians on the shared caseFinanceService path; (2) the UPDATE split removes the RLS-level creator-self-approval backstop. Tenant isolation and append-only ledger/audit tables are untouched. Apply only after the two must-fix policy corrections; MAIN-LOOP-ONLY via mcp apply_migration, then regen database.types.ts (though no column change, the manifest/types pipeline should still run).

item.title: UI approver gate (admin||accounts) diverges from RLS (owner/admin/manager/accounts); hardcoded role strings
item.scope: Decide canonical approver set, converge UI and RLS, replace hardcoded role literals in ExpensesList with PermissionsContext per CLAUDE.md.

design.title: Converge expense approver UI gate with RLS (owner/admin/manager/accounts) and remove hardcoded role-string literals
design.summary:
EXP-012 is a frontend-only authorization-divergence fix. The live RLS for the expense mutate path is `expenses_update` / `expenses_insert` = `has_role('accounts')`, which (per the live `has_role` definition I read) resolves to the hierarchical set {owner, admin, manager, accounts}. But `ExpensesList.tsx` hardcodes `isAccountsRole = profile?.role === 'admin' || profile?.role === 'accounts'` (line 126) and `canBulkArchive = profile?.role === 'owner' || profile?.role === 'admin'` (line 96), gating the Approve/Reject (line 675), Mark-as-Paid (line 693) affordances. Consequences observed in code: (1) owner and manager users — who CAN approve/reject/mark-paid per RLS and via the service functions directly — never see those buttons, so the on-screen control list is untrustworthy and they have hidden powers; (2) the component uses raw role-string literals instead of a permissions abstraction, which CLAUDE.md forbids ("Never hardcode role strings in components — use the permission system").

The safe, correct scope: introduce ONE canonical client-side helper that mirrors the `has_role('accounts')` server set — {owner, admin, manager, accounts} — and use it to gate the approver affordances; converge `isAccountsRole` to that set. This makes the UI exactly match what RLS permits, so the buttons no longer lie. The SoD guard (creator ≠ approver) already lives server-side in approveExpense (lines 402-405, EXP-008, shipped), so broadening the visible approver set introduces NO new self-approval risk: an owner/manager who created the expense will still be rejected by the service with "You cannot approve an expense you created", surfaced via the existing onError toast. No new approval power is granted that RLS+service don't already allow — this only makes the affordance visible to users who already had the capability.

Bulk-archive (`canBulkArchive`, owner/admin) is intentionally NARROWER than the soft-delete UPDATE RLS and is the subject of a separate finding (EXP-018, which wants archive TIGHTENED to admin via RLS/RPC, not broadened). Therefore EXP-012's scope explicitly EXCLUDES touching canBulkArchive — changing it here would collide with EXP-018's intended direction. I replace only the literal it's built from with the shared helper's building blocks where it improves clarity, but keep its role set unchanged.

Grounding: I read the live RLS policies on public.expenses, the live has_role(text) function body, expensesService.ts (approve/reject/markPaid all already status- and SoD-guarded server-side), ExpensesList.tsx (the two hardcoded gates), PermissionsContext.tsx (module-level only — no per-action 'approve expense' key exists, confirmed by memory obs 8876), and roles.ts (note: roles.ts omits 'manager' from its ROLES tuple even though the live role hierarchy and profiles include it — so a typed helper must accept the broader string set, not be keyed to roles.ts's Role type).

3) Update the two affordance gates that referenced `isAccountsRole`: line 675 `{expense.status === 'pending' && isAccountsRole && (` -> `&& canManage &&`; line 693 `{expense.status === 'approved' && isAccountsRole && (` -> `&& canManage &&`. These are the Approve/Reject pair and the Mark-as-Paid button.
4) Fix the empty approve-confirm Modal title (the (a) half of finding §EXP-012 / line 220-228): line 785 `title=""` -> `title="Approve Expense"` so the dialog has a descriptive accessible name instead of falling back to 'Dialog'. (Same finding row; cheap to ship together.)
5) Do NOT change `canBulkArchive` (line 96) or the bulk-archive gate (line 747) — that set is owner/admin by intent and is governed by EXP-018, which wants it tightened, not widened. No behavior change to the mutations themselves — approveExpense/rejectExpense/recordExpenseDisbursement already enforce status + SoD server-side; this only makes the buttons visible to the owner/manager who already had the capability.
