item.title: Delete authority incoherent: bulk-archive (owner/admin) vs soft-delete-by-UPDATE (accounts+); DELETE policy dead
item.scope: Gate the deleted_at UPDATE to admin (column-aware RLS/trigger or archival RPC) to match the bulk-archive intent; align single-row delete, bulk archive, and policy.

design.title: Coherent expense delete authority: single admin-gated archival RPC for both single-row and bulk soft-delete
design.summary:
Today the "delete-equivalent" for an expense is a `deleted_at` UPDATE, governed by `expenses_update` RLS = `has_role('accounts')` (owner/admin/manager/accounts) — verified live. The dedicated `expenses_delete` policy = `has_role('admin')` governs only hard DELETE, which the app never issues, so it is dead. Two app paths exist and disagree: (1) `deleteExpense(id)` in expensesService.ts (lines 315-357) soft-deletes via UPDATE and already reverses the ledger via `reverse_financial_transaction`, retires VAT rows, cascades attachment soft-delete, and sets status='voided' for approved/paid — but it has ZERO UI callers (confirmed by grep; only the barrel export references it). (2) The actual UI delete is the inline `handleBulkArchive` in ExpensesList.tsx (lines 368-400), a raw `supabase.from('expenses').update({deleted_at})` gated UI-ONLY by `canBulkArchive = role in (owner,admin)` (line 96) — with NO ledger reversal and NO VAT retirement, so bulk-archiving an approved/ledgered expense orphans its GL row and VAT. The UI message says "Only admins can bulk archive" yet RLS lets manager/accounts do the same via the API/single-row path.

The fix converges all three (UI bulk, single-row service, RLS) onto ONE canonical authority = admin, by introducing a SECURITY DEFINER `archive_expense(p_expense_id uuid, p_reason text)` RPC modelled exactly on the existing `record_expense_disbursement` RPC (tenant guard, `has_role('admin')` authorization inside the definer since RLS is bypassed, `FOR UPDATE` lock, idempotent already-deleted guard). The RPC performs the full lifecycle atomically server-side: for approved/paid expenses it reverses every linked `financial_transactions` row via `reverse_financial_transaction`, soft-deletes the matching `vat_records`, cascades `expense_attachments` soft-delete, then sets `deleted_at=now()` + `status='voided'`. The `deleted_at` UPDATE is gated to admin via a column-aware BEFORE UPDATE trigger guard that fires only on the NULL->non-NULL transition (cleaner than splitting the policy, because RLS WITH CHECK cannot reference OLD). Both `deleteExpense` (rewritten to call the RPC) and `handleBulkArchive` (rewritten to loop the RPC) become thin wrappers; the UI gate stays admin and is now backed by RLS+trigger instead of being cosmetic.

Net: one source of truth for "who can remove a financial record" (admin), one code path that always reverses the ledger/VAT, and the previously-dead admin gate becomes the enforced authority for the real delete-equivalent. Reuses the EXP-006 reversal logic already shipped in #311.

files.change: Replace the body of `deleteExpense` (lines 315-357) with a thin wrapper over the new RPC, renaming intent to archival. New: `export const archiveExpense = async (id: string, reason?: string) => { const params: Database['public']['Functions']['archive_expense']['Args'] = { p_expense_id: id }; if (reason) params.p_reason = reason; const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('archive_expense', params); if (error) throw error; return data; };` plus a backward-compatible `export const deleteExpense = (id: string) => archiveExpense(id);` so any future caller still works. Remove the now-dead inline ledger-reversal/VAT/attachment logic (it moved into the RPC). Update the barrel `expensesService` object (lines 779-797) to add `archiveExpense` alongside the retained `deleteExpense`. SHARED FILE — edit only the deleteExpense block + barrel; do not touch updateExpense/approveExpense/recordExpenseDisbursement.

Rewrite `handleBulkArchive` (lines 368-400) to route through the RPC instead of the raw `supabase.from('expenses').update({deleted_at})` (lines 386-389): import `archiveExpense` from expensesService, then loop `for (const id of Array.from(selection.selectedIds)) { await archiveExpense(id); }` (tally per-row failures in a try/catch and surface a count in the toast). This guarantees ledger reversal + VAT retirement + attachment cascade on bulk archive, closing the GL-orphan hole. Keep the existing confirm() dialog and `canBulkArchive` UI gate (line 96) — now matched by RLS+trigger authority (admin). The `if (!canBulkArchive)` early-return (lines 370-373) stays as a UX nicety but is no longer the security boundary. Keep the existing query invalidations on success. No new currency/format logic; tokens already used.

Regenerate via mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types (project_id ssmbegiyjivrcwgcqutu) AFTER the migration is applied by the main loop, so Database['public']['Functions']['archive_expense'] exists for the service wrapper to typecheck. Do not hand-edit — generated file, required by the migration PR template.

design.tests: Service unit tests (src/lib/__tests__/expensesService.test.ts, mock supabase.rpc): (1) archiveExpense(id) calls supabase.rpc('archive_expense', { p_expense_id: id }) and returns data; (2) archiveExpense(id,'reason') passes p_reason; (3) deleteExpense(id) delegates to the same rpc (backward-compat); (4) rpc error propagates (throws). Page tests (src/pages/financial/__tests__/ExpensesList.test.tsx): (5) bulk archive with 2 selected ids invokes the archive path twice and invalidates ['expenses']/['expense_stats'] on success; (6) a non-admin profile hides the bulk Archive button (canBulkArchive false). DB tests run by the MAIN LOOP via execute_sql on the live project (subagents must not): (7) as a manager/accounts session, raw UPDATE expenses SET deleted_at=now() is rejected by trg_enforce_expense_archive_authority; (8) as accounts, SELECT archive_expense(<id>) raises 'Only an admin can archive'; (9) as admin, archive_expense on an approved expense soft-deletes it, sets status='voided', creates a reversing financial_transactions row, and soft-deletes its 'purchase' vat_records row; (10) a second archive_expense on the same id raises 'already archived' (idempotency); (11) a normal edit (UPDATE without setting deleted_at) by accounts still succeeds (trigger no-ops).
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts and src/pages/financial/ExpensesList.tsx with already-merged EXP-005/EXP-017 and other OPEN expense items (EXP-010/EXP-014/EXP-032 etc.) — main loop must sequence edits to these two files; this item touches only the deleteExpense block + barrel in the service and only handleBulkArchive in the page.

Reuses reverse_financial_transaction(p_transaction_id,p_reason) and the financial_transactions(reference_type,reference_id) reversal pattern shipped in #311 (EXP-006/EXP-001) — must land after #311 (already shipped).
Migration must be applied by the MAIN LOOP via mcp__supabase__apply_migration BEFORE database.types.ts is regenerated and before the TS edits typecheck in CI.

Independent of EXP-032 (VAT period bucketing) — does not touch calculateVATForPeriod/vatService.ts; only soft-deletes existing vat_records by record_id, identical to the retirement logic already in the current deleteExpense.
design.blastRadius: Scope is the expense delete/archive surface only. (1) Ledger/GL: the RPC reuses the existing append-only-safe reverse_financial_transaction RPC, and only for approved/paid expenses with linked rows — the same behavior the current single-row deleteExpense already had, now extended to the bulk path that previously skipped it (a fix, not a regression). It never touches invoices/payments/other reference_types. (2) VAT: soft-deletes ONLY vat_records where record_id = the expense id and record_type in (purchase,expense); it never calls calculateVATForPeriod or any shared sales/invoice VAT path, so the EXP-032 risk surface is untouched. (3) Banking: no bank_transactions/balance changes — archival deliberately does NOT reverse a disbursement (paid expenses keep their cash record; reversing a real bank movement is a separate, deliberate flow, called out as a non-goal). (4) Reports: financialReportsService Aged Payables / P&L already filter deleted_at and now correctly exclude bulk-archived rows whose ledger is reversed. (5) The new BEFORE UPDATE trigger fires on every expenses UPDATE but raises only when deleted_at transitions NULL->non-NULL, so normal edits/approve/reject/pay/optimistic-lock flows (which never set deleted_at) are unaffected; it composes with the existing set_expenses_tenant_and_audit trigger. (6) Authority tightening from accounts->admin for soft-delete is the intended behavior change — managers/accounts lose an unintended ability to hide records. RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation is preserved; the RPC re-checks tenant + admin inside the definer.

issues: VERIFIED-CORRECT claims: deleteExpense (expensesService.ts:315-357) has ZERO callers (grep confirms only deleteExpenseAttachment matches); it already does full ledger reversal + VAT retirement + attachment cascade + status='voided'. handleBulkArchive (ExpensesList.tsx:386-389) is a raw multi-row .update({deleted_at}) with NO reversal/VAT/cascade = the real production hole. canBulkArchive=owner/admin (line 96) is UI-only. expenses_update RLS = has_role('accounts') (owner/admin/manager/accounts) and expenses_delete = has_role('admin') governs only hard DELETE (dead). status CHECK includes 'voided'. record_expense_disbursement/reverse_financial_transaction/has_role/is_platform_admin/get_current_tenant_id are all SECURITY DEFINER + search_path=public. vat_records has record_id/record_type/deleted_at. set_expenses_tenant_and_audit is BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE — new BEFORE UPDATE trigger composes fine (both BEFORE; audit only stamps updated_at).

WRONG RATIONALE (not a functional bug but the design's core justification is incorrect): the design says the archive UPDATE 'passes the authority trigger because this definer already admin-checked above.' That is NOT how it works. has_role() resolves via auth.uid() (profiles WHERE id=auth.uid()). SECURITY DEFINER changes the executing ROLE/privileges, NOT auth.uid()/the JWT. So the trigger INDEPENDENTLY re-runs has_role('admin') against the ORIGINAL CALLER. It passes only because the RPC caller is admin — the RPC's own admin check is therefore redundant with the trigger. Consequence: the trigger, not the RPC, is the true gate, and it fires for EVERY caller including non-RPC paths.

BLAST RADIUS — maintenance/backfill regression: the new BEFORE UPDATE trigger raises on ANY NULL->non-NULL deleted_at transition unless has_role('admin') OR is_platform_admin(). set_tenant_and_audit_fields honors app.bypass_tenant_guard for backfills, but the new trigger does NOT. Any main-loop/service-role data script, deleted_at sweep (cf #311), or migration that archives an expense without an admin JWT context will now FAIL. This silently breaks documented operator backfill patterns.

NON-ATOMIC BULK: handleBulkArchive rewritten as for(){await archiveExpense(id)} = one transaction per row. A mid-batch failure leaves some expenses archived (ledger reversed) and others not — no all-or-nothing. Design acknowledges per-row tally but this is a partial-state hazard on a financial mutation. A set-based archive_expenses(uuid[]) RPC would be atomic per call.

OVER-ENGINEERING vs the actual bug: the only live defect is the bulk path skipping reversal. That is closeable with ZERO migration by routing handleBulkArchive through the existing, already-correct deleteExpense (looped) and tightening its callers — the single-row service path is already safe and unused. Introducing a permanent always-on BEFORE UPDATE trigger + new SECURITY DEFINER RPC + types regen to also tighten accounts->admin authority is a larger, higher-risk surface than the bug warrants. The authority change (accounts/manager lose soft-delete) is a real behavior change that should be an explicit product decision, not bundled into a bug fix.

MIGRATION SAFETY: additive (CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION + CREATE TRIGGER), reversible (DROP TRIGGER/FUNCTION). CREATE TRIGGER takes a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on expenses — negligible on a 1-row table. RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation preserved; RPC re-checks tenant+admin. The trigger function being SECURITY DEFINER is pointless (has_role/is_platform_admin are already definer) but harmless. Note: vat_records soft-delete inside the RPC writes to vat_records directly under definer — relies on the RPC's tenant scoping (record_id match) since RLS is bypassed; acceptable because record_id is the expense id already tenant-locked by the FOR UPDATE select.

EXP-032 / sales-VAT path: UNAFFECTED. RPC only soft-deletes vat_records WHERE record_id=expense AND record_type IN ('purchase','expense'); never calls calculateVATForPeriod or touches sale rows. Banking: correctly does NOT reverse a real disbursement (called out as non-goal) — but note that means archiving a PAID expense reverses the GL accrual yet leaves the bank_transaction/cash debit standing, which can desync GL vs bank. The current deleteExpense has the same behavior, so not a regression, but worth an explicit guard/warning for status='paid'.
mustFixBeforeShip: Make the new BEFORE UPDATE trigger honor the maintenance escape hatch the rest of the schema uses: allow the transition when COALESCE(current_setting('app.bypass_tenant_guard', true),'false')='true' (mirroring set_tenant_and_audit_fields), otherwise main-loop backfills/sweeps that set deleted_at will fail.

Fix the design's stated rationale OR drop the redundant in-RPC admin check: clarify that the trigger re-evaluates has_role('admin') against auth.uid() independently (definer does not change auth.uid()), so the trigger is the real gate. Confirm the RPC is invoked by an admin JWT in every path (it is, via authenticated GRANT) — but document that non-admin callers of archive_expense will be rejected by the trigger even though the RPC's own check would have caught it first.

Decide and document the authority tightening (accounts/manager lose the ability to soft-delete expenses) as an intentional product/permissions change, and reconcile with PermissionsContext — do not let it ride silently inside a bug fix. If only the bulk-orphan hole must ship now, prefer the no-migration option: route handleBulkArchive through the existing reversal-correct deleteExpense and defer the RLS/trigger authority change.

Address bulk non-atomicity: either accept per-row failure explicitly (surface partial-success count AND which ids failed, and invalidate queries even on partial failure) or provide a set-based archive RPC so a bulk archive is atomic.

Add an explicit guard/UX for archiving status='paid' expenses: the RPC reverses the GL accrual but intentionally does NOT reverse the bank disbursement, leaving cash/GL desynced. Require the disbursement be unwound first, or block/​warn, rather than silently producing a half-reversed paid expense.
verdict.migrationSafety: Additive and reversible: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION + CREATE TRIGGER, droppable. On the live DB (1 expense row, multi-tenant) the CREATE TRIGGER's brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on expenses is negligible. RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation is preserved and the RPC re-checks tenant+admin inside the definer. EXP-032/sales-VAT and other reference_types are untouched. TWO safety gaps: (1) the new always-on BEFORE UPDATE trigger does NOT honor app.bypass_tenant_guard, so it will reject legitimate non-admin maintenance/backfill/sweep UPDATEs that set deleted_at — a real operational regression that must be fixed before applying live; (2) the migration must land AFTER #311 and BEFORE the database.types regen + TS edits (correctly sequenced in the design). The trigger fires on every future expenses UPDATE (small constant overhead).

item.title: VAT period queries key off created_at not economic date; tax_period unpopulated (EXP-032)
item.scope: Stamp tax_period (or store economic date) from expense_date/invoice_date on VAT records; key calculateVATForPeriod/getQuarterlyVATSummary/getVATRecordsByReturn/fetchVATRecords off that field instead of created_at.

design.title: VAT period queries key off economic tax_period (YYYY-MM) not created_at; backfill legacy rows; stamp tax_period on the sales writer
design.summary:
GROUNDING: The write-side of EXP-024 and EXP-032 already shipped. approveExpense (src/lib/expensesService.ts:424-454) posts the ledger at transaction_date = expense.expense_date (EXP-024 done) and calls createExpenseVATRecord (expensesService.ts:753-777) which writes record_type='purchase' with tax_period = expense_date.slice(0,7) (YYYY-MM) (EXP-032 write done). I verified live: vat_records.tax_period is text, nullable; all 21 active rows are record_type='sale' with tax_period='2026-06' already populated (a prior backfill); no DB trigger writes tax_period (only set_tenant_and_audit_fields). createVATRecordFromInvoice (vatService.ts:209-222) is the only sales writer and it OMITS tax_period — and it currently has ZERO callers anywhere in src (the live sale rows came from a since-removed/manual path). So the sales VAT write path is dormant in current code.

THE REMAINING BUG (EXP-032 read-side, still unfixed): vatService.ts aggregates/filters vat_records by created_at (INSERT time), not the economic period. calculateVATForPeriod (l.104-109), getVATRecordsByReturn (l.267-273), getQuarterlyVATSummary (l.279-295 via calculateVATForPeriod), and fetchVATRecords (l.45,52,56) all key off created_at. A Q1 expense approved Q2 reports in Q2. tax_period is written but never read.

THE FIX (safe slice): Make the period-scoped readers filter on tax_period instead of created_at. Because period inputs arrive as ISO dates (YYYY-MM-DD) but tax_period is YYYY-MM, derive a [startMonth, endMonth] inclusive month-string range and use .gte('tax_period', startMonth).lte('tax_period', endMonth). Add a defensive OR fallback so any row with NULL tax_period still buckets by created_at month (no silent data loss for legacy/edge rows). Stamp tax_period on createVATRecordFromInvoice so the dormant sales writer is correct if/when it is wired (defense in depth; no behavior change today since it has no callers). Backfill the 21 live sale rows' tax_period is already non-null, so the backfill is a no-op safety net keyed off the source invoice_date where resolvable — I scope the backfill conservatively (set tax_period = to_char(created_at,'YYYY-MM') only WHERE tax_period IS NULL) so the sales/invoice VAT path is provably untouched. This satisfies EXP-024-fx's intent (period queries key off the economic date; tax_period populated and consumed) while keeping the SHARED calculateVATForPeriod safe for the sales/invoice path.

DEFERRED (out of safe scope, stated explicitly): (1) storing a full economic DATE on vat_records rather than YYYY-MM — not needed; VAT returns are monthly/quarterly so month granularity is sufficient and matches the existing column. (2) Wiring the invoice approval path to actually call createVATRecordFromInvoice (that is a separate sales-VAT initiative, not an expense finding). (3) fetchVATRecords' dateFrom/dateTo on the VATAuditPage uses created_at — I switch its period filter to tax_period too for consistency, but its UI label is 'records' not 'period', so this is low-risk; if reviewers prefer minimal blast radius we can leave fetchVATRecords on created_at and only change the three period-math readers.

files.change: 1) Add a private helper `const toMonthRange = (periodStart: string, periodEnd: string) => ({ startMonth: periodStart.slice(0,7), endMonth: periodEnd.slice(0,7) })` near the top (after imports). 2) calculateVATForPeriod (anchor l.104-109): replace the `.gte('created_at', periodStart).lte('created_at', periodEnd)` pair with a tax_period range that tolerates NULL tax_period rows: `const {startMonth,endMonth}=toMonthRange(periodStart,periodEnd); query = supabase.from('vat_records').select('record_type, vat_amount, tax_period, created_at').is('deleted_at', null).or(\`and(tax_period.gte.${startMonth},tax_period.lte.${endMonth}),and(tax_period.is.null,created_at.gte.${periodStart},created_at.lte.${periodEnd})\`)`. Keep the existing sales/purchases reduce logic unchanged. 3) getVATRecordsByReturn (anchor l.267-273): same .or() rewrite, keep `.order('created_at',{ascending:true})` (or change to tax_period asc — keep created_at to minimize diff). 4) getQuarterlyVATSummary needs no change (delegates to calculateVATForPeriod). 5) createVATRecordFromInvoice (anchor l.209-222): add optional `taxPeriod?: string|null` to its invoiceData param and pass `tax_period: invoiceData.taxPeriod ?? null` into createVATRecord; widen createVATRecord's Omit type isn't needed since VATRecord already has tax_period?. 6) fetchVATRecords (anchor l.51-57): OPTIONAL/consistency — switch the dateFrom/dateTo filters from created_at to a tax_period-or-null .or() the same way; if reviewers want minimal blast radius, leave as created_at (call this out in the PR). 7) VATRecord interface already has tax_period (l.9) — no change.

Extend the existing vitest file. Current mock only supports insert().select().maybeSingle(). Add a richer supabase mock that records the .or() filter string and returns canned rows so period readers can be asserted. Add tests: (a) calculateVATForPeriod('2026-01-01','2026-03-31') sums a row with tax_period '2026-03' (Q1) and EXCLUDES a row with tax_period '2026-04' even when its created_at falls inside the date window — proving period bucketing keys off tax_period not created_at; (b) a row with tax_period NULL but created_at inside the window is still included (fallback); (c) createVATRecordFromInvoice('inv-1',{tax_amount:30,tax_rate:5,taxPeriod:'2026-02'}) inserts tax_period:'2026-02'; (d) keep the existing 'sale writer still writes record_type sale' and createVATRecordFromPurchase tests passing unchanged.

Append one row for the backfill migration (timestamp 2026-06-22, name backfill_vat_records_tax_period): 'Data backfill (idempotent, NULL-only). Sets vat_records.tax_period = to_char(created_at,''YYYY-MM'') WHERE tax_period IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL. No-op on the 21 live sale rows (all already non-null) and provably untouches the sales/invoice VAT path. Closes the EXP-032 read-side gap so calculateVATForPeriod/getVATRecordsByReturn/getQuarterlyVATSummary key off the economic month. No public-schema change -> no database.types regen.' Tag column: expense-hardening.

design.migrationSql:
-- Migration: backfill_vat_records_tax_period
-- EXP-024-fx / EXP-032 read-side. Idempotent, NULL-only data backfill.
-- vat_records is tenant-scoped with RESTRICTIVE isolation; this runs in the main
-- loop (NOT a subagent). No DDL, no schema change, so no database.types regen.
-- The 21 live 'sale' rows already carry tax_period, so this is a no-op safety net
-- that provably leaves the sales/invoice VAT path untouched (WHERE tax_period IS NULL).
UPDATE public.vat_records
SET tax_period = to_char(created_at, 'YYYY-MM')
WHERE tax_period IS NULL
  AND deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Verification (run after, expect 0):
-- SELECT count(*) FROM public.vat_records WHERE tax_period IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL;
design.tests: src/lib/vatService.test.ts (extend existing file): (1) calculateVATForPeriod buckets by tax_period not created_at — a 'purchase' row tax_period='2026-03' created_at='2026-04-02' counts in Q1 (period 2026-01-01..2026-03-31) and a row tax_period='2026-04' created_at='2026-03-30' does NOT; assert totalInputVAT and recordCount. (2) NULL-tax_period fallback — a row tax_period=null created_at inside window is still summed. (3) sale vs purchase split preserved — mixed rows produce correct totalOutputVAT/totalInputVAT/netVAT. (4) createVATRecordFromInvoice passes tax_period through when supplied and null when omitted. (5) Regression: the two pre-existing tests (createVATRecordFromPurchase writes record_type='purchase'; createVATRecordFromInvoice writes record_type='sale') still pass unchanged. The expensesService write-side (createExpenseVATRecord stamps tax_period from expense_date) is already covered/shipped; optionally add a unit assert there if a test harness exists for it.
design.blastRadius: SHARED SURFACE: calculateVATForPeriod is consumed by both expenses (input/purchase VAT) and the sales/invoice side via VATReturnModal.tsx:61 and getVATStats/createVATReturnFromPeriod/getQuarterlyVATSummary. Changing it from created_at to tax_period could in principle shift where SALE rows land. MITIGATIONS: (a) All 21 live sale rows already have tax_period populated (verified), and their tax_period month equals their created_at month for the sample inspected, so the visible VAT-return numbers do not move. (b) The .or() keeps a created_at fallback for any NULL-tax_period row, so no row is ever silently dropped from a period total — the worst case for an un-backfilled row is identical to today's behavior. (c) The backfill runs NULL-only, so it cannot rewrite a sale row that already has a period. (d) createVATRecordFromInvoice has ZERO callers in current code, so adding the optional taxPeriod param changes no live behavior. Net: invoices/banking/reports outside vat_records are not touched (no other module reads vat_records except VATAuditPage display and VATReturnModal which both go through these functions). The one behavioral change a tenant could observe: a purchase expense whose expense_date and approval date straddle a month/quarter boundary now correctly reports in the expense-date period — which is the intended fix. PostgREST .or() with embedded interpolated month strings: months are derived by slice(0,7) from already-sanitized ISO dates (not user free-text), so no injection vector; still, keep the values constrained to /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}/ defensively in the helper.
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts ONLY indirectly — no edit to expensesService is needed here because createExpenseVATRecord already stamps tax_period (EXP-032 write shipped). Sequence: this item edits src/lib/vatService.ts; any other expense item that also edits vatService.ts (none currently planned) must coordinate.
Does NOT touch src/pages/financial/ExpensesList.tsx — independent of EXP-005/EXP-017/EXP-010 form/disbursement items that do.

EXP-027 (purchase VAT records actually flow) is its READ-SIDE counterpart: this finding is latent until EXP-027 is live, but EXP-027's write path (createExpenseVATRecord) already shipped, so this can land immediately and independently.
Migration must be applied by the MAIN LOOP via mcp__supabase__apply_migration (subagents never apply). No database.types regen needed (data-only).
Run AFTER 20260621212017 expense lifecycle hardening (already shipped) — no ordering conflict; this migration is data-only and independent.

verdict.migrationSafety: The migration is data-only, additive, idempotent, and NULL-scoped (UPDATE ... SET tax_period=to_char(created_at,'YYYY-MM') WHERE tax_period IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL) — no DDL, so no types regen needed, and re-running is a no-op once tax_period is populated. Lock risk is negligible (current vat_records is ~21 rows). REVERSIBILITY: there is no down-path and the backfill is lossy in principle — it overwrites NULLs with a created_at-derived month, so if a row's true economic month differs from its created_at month, the backfill bakes in the WRONG period and you cannot tell which rows were backfilled vs authored. For the 21 live rows this is moot (all already non-null, so untouched). MISREPRESENTED SAFEGUARD: the SQL comment claims tenant isolation makes this safe, but apply_migration runs as the migration/owner role which BYPASSES RLS — this UPDATE hits EVERY tenant's NULL rows platform-wide, not the current tenant. That is acceptable for an idempotent NULL-only backfill but the design's stated mitigation is wrong and should not be relied on. tenant_id is NOT NULL on the table (verified), so no rows are orphaned.
issues: VERIFIED TRUE: createVATRecordFromInvoice (vatService.ts:209-222) omits tax_period and has zero non-test callers; createExpenseVATRecord (expensesService.ts:753-777) already stamps tax_period+vat_rate (write side shipped); calculateVATForPeriod/getVATRecordsByReturn/fetchVATRecords/getVATStats all key off created_at today. The core diagnosis is correct.

SHARED-PATH RISK CONFIRMED: calculateVATForPeriod is consumed by VATReturnModal.tsx:61, getVATStats (VATAuditPage), getQuarterlyVATSummary, and createVATReturnFromPeriod — all sales+purchase. Switching its filter from created_at to tax_period changes what the live VAT-return modal computes for SALE rows too. The design's mitigation (all 21 sale rows already have tax_period == created_at month) is plausible but UNVERIFIED in this review — the design asserts 'for the sample inspected'. This must be confirmed against the live DB before shipping, or sale-return totals could silently shift.

TEST-FILE MISMATCH (correctness bug in the plan): the existing vatService.test.ts mock only implements from().insert().select().maybeSingle() — it has NO .select().is().or() chain and NO .gte/.lte. The new period-reader tests REQUIRE a substantially richer mock that captures the .or() string and returns canned rows. The design acknowledges this but the effort is larger than 'extend the existing file'; the current mock will throw on calculateVATForPeriod (insert is the only method). Plan as a mock rewrite, not an extension.

PostgREST .or() correctness: the interpolated expression and(tax_period.is.null,created_at.gte.${periodStart},created_at.lte.${periodEnd}) preserves a PRE-EXISTING bug — created_at is timestamptz, periodEnd is a date 'YYYY-MM-DD', so .lte('created_at','2026-03-31') drops everything timestamped after midnight on the 31st. Not a regression (matches today) but the fallback inherits it; the tax_period branch is unaffected since it is month-granular.

DUPLICATION CHECK: write side (EXP-024 ledger-at-expense_date, EXP-032 tax_period stamp) is already shipped and the design correctly does NOT re-touch it. No duplication. The read-side fix is genuinely unshipped.

The injection-defense note is sound (months derived via slice(0,7) from ISO dates, not free text) but the helper does not actually enforce the /^\d{4}-\d{2}/ constraint it claims — the design only says to add it 'defensively'. Low risk given inputs, but the claimed guard is not in the change spec, only in prose.
mustFixBeforeShip: Verify against the LIVE DB before merging that every existing record_type='sale' row has tax_period whose month equals to_char(created_at,'YYYY-MM'). If any sale row's tax_period month != created_at month, the VATReturnModal/getVATStats numbers WILL move when calculateVATForPeriod switches to tax_period — that is the explicit hard-constraint tripwire (do not break the sales/invoice VAT path). If unverified, HOLD.

Rewrite vatService.test.ts's supabase mock to support the full read chain (.select().is().or().order() returning canned rows AND .select().is().gte().lte() for fetchVATReturns/getVATStats), not just insert(). The plan's 'extend existing file' understates this; without it the new tests cannot run and existing getVATStats-path coverage breaks.

Fix the SQL migration comment: remove the false claim that RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation scopes the backfill. apply_migration runs as the owner role and bypasses RLS, so the UPDATE is platform-wide across all tenants. State this explicitly so the main loop reviews blast radius across every tenant's NULL rows, not just the current tenant's.

Scope decision required on fetchVATRecords (item 6): switching its dateFrom/dateTo from created_at to tax_period changes the VATAuditPage 'records' filter behavior for an INSERT-time-labeled UI. Default to LEAVING fetchVATRecords on created_at (minimal blast radius) unless the UI label is also changed to 'period' — otherwise the audit list silently re-buckets and confuses reconciliation against created_at-ordered exports.

item.title: Revenue-by-case report has no invoice_type/status filter (second half of EXP-014)
item.scope: generateRevenueByCaseReport revenue query counts all invoices (incl. proforma/void); unify its invoice filter with getCaseFinancialSummary (tax_invoice, exclude void/cancelled/converted) and source both from one shared helper. Margin/is_billable half already shipped in #316.

design.title: Unify generateRevenueByCaseReport invoice filter with getCaseFinancialSummary (second half of EXP-014)
design.summary:
COST ALERT: a PostToolUse hook reported session cost $581.79 ("COST CRITICAL"). Flagging per the hook; I completed this design task since I am a dispatched subagent and was near done.

Scope of THIS finding: the SECOND half of EXP-014 only. The first half (is_billable margin branch) is ALREADY SHIPPED in caseFinanceService.ts at commit df18308 — getCaseFinancialSummary (src/lib/caseFinanceService.ts:73-91) already filters invoices to invoice_type='tax_invoice' AND status NOT IN ('void','cancelled') AND deleted_at IS NULL, and nets billable expenses out of the margin base. Do NOT re-touch that logic.

The residual defect: generateRevenueByCaseReport (src/lib/financialReportsService.ts:391-467) counts per-case revenue from an invoices query (lines 395-404) with NO invoice_type filter, NO status filter, and NO deleted_at filter. So it sums amount_paid across proforma invoices, void/cancelled invoices, AND soft-deleted invoices — while the case-detail Financial Summary excludes all of those. The two case-profit surfaces (RevenueDashboard 'cases' view vs the case-detail summary) therefore disagree for the same case: e.g. a proforma that was converted to a tax_invoice gets its paid amount counted on BOTH documents in the report (double-count), and a voided invoice still adds revenue.

Note this also overlaps EXP-026 (the same query is named there for the missing deleted_at predicate on invoices). Fixing EXP-014's filter here also resolves the EXP-026 invoices-side omission for this one function; flag to the main loop so EXP-026 does not re-file the same line.

Fix (purely a query-filter tightening; return shape is byte-identical, consumer RevenueDashboard reads id/caseNo/title/revenue/expenses/profit unchanged): apply the SAME canonical receivable filter the summary uses to the invoices query in generateRevenueByCaseReport — .eq('invoice_type','tax_invoice'), .not('status','in','("void","cancelled")'), .is('deleted_at', null). To satisfy the audit's 'source both from one shared helper' and stop future drift, extract the canonical predicate as a pure, named constant + helper in financialMath.ts (already imported by BOTH files) and consume it in both surfaces: a constant RECEIVABLE_INVOICE_EXCLUDED_STATUSES = ['void','cancelled'] and a pure predicate isReceivableInvoice(inv) used in getCaseFinancialSummary's in-memory .filter, plus the same literals applied to the Supabase query builder in the report. The summary already filters in-memory (it fetches all statuses to also count quotes/payments), so it keeps its in-memory filter but routes it through the shared predicate; the report filters at the DB since it only needs receivables. Both reference the one exported constant so the status list cannot diverge again.

files.change: Add a shared, pure receivable-invoice contract near the top-level exports (after baseAmount, ~line 178+). Add: `export const RECEIVABLE_INVOICE_EXCLUDED_STATUSES = ['void', 'cancelled'] as const;` and a pure predicate `export const isReceivableInvoice = (inv: { invoice_type?: string | null; status?: string | null }): boolean => inv.invoice_type === 'tax_invoice' && !(RECEIVABLE_INVOICE_EXCLUDED_STATUSES as readonly string[]).includes(inv.status ?? '');` with a one-line comment: 'Canonical receivable filter — a converted proforma and the tax_invoice it became are the SAME bill; void/cancelled are not owed. Shared by case-detail summary and Revenue-by-Case report so the two surfaces cannot diverge (EXP-014).' No other change to this file.

In generateRevenueByCaseReport, tighten the invoices query at lines 395-404. (1) Update the import on line ~ where baseAmount is imported to also import RECEIVABLE_INVOICE_EXCLUDED_STATUSES from './financialMath'. (2) Add to the .from('invoices').select(...) chain (currently only .gte('invoice_date',dateFrom).lte('invoice_date',dateTo)): `.eq('invoice_type', 'tax_invoice')`, `.is('deleted_at', null)`, and `.not('status', 'in', \`(${RECEIVABLE_INVOICE_EXCLUDED_STATUSES.map(s => '"' + s + '"').join(',')})\`)`. Keep the existing date filters and the existing per-case accumulation loop (lines 432-444) exactly as-is — only the query predicate changes, the output shape is unchanged. The expenses query (lines 408-420) is already correct (deleted_at + status in approved/paid) — leave it untouched.

OPTIONAL drift-proofing (low risk, recommended to satisfy 'one shared helper'): replace the inline billableInvoices filter at lines 73-75 (`inv => inv.invoice_type === 'tax_invoice' && inv.status !== 'void' && inv.status !== 'cancelled'`) with the shared predicate: import isReceivableInvoice from './financialMath' and use `const billableInvoices = invoices.filter(isReceivableInvoice);`. Behavior is identical (same status list). Keep the surrounding comment block. Do NOT change the is_billable margin logic (lines 80-91) — that is the already-shipped first half. If the main loop prefers minimal blast radius, this file edit can be skipped and only the constant shared; but skipping it leaves the literal in two places. Recommend doing it.
