item.title: expenses_select is USING(true): every staff role incl. viewer sees all expenses tenant-wide
item.scope: Gate SELECT behind is_staff_user() at minimum (block viewers), or submitter-scope via created_by=auth.uid() OR has_role('manager'/'accounts'); add mine/all toggle and document the visibility model.

design.title: Gate expenses_select behind is_staff_user() so viewers can't read tenant-wide expense financials
design.summary:
The PERMISSIVE `expenses_select` policy on `public.expenses` is `USING(true)` (verified live), ANDed only with the RESTRICTIVE `expenses_tenant_isolation`. Result: any authenticated tenant principal — including read-only `viewer` and non-financial staff — can read every staff member's expense rows (amounts, vendors, descriptions, notes, case links). The only role gate today is on the UI action buttons (`isAccountsRole` in ExpensesList.tsx:126), not on visibility.

SAFE SLICE (this design): replace `expenses_select` `USING(true)` with `is_staff_user() OR is_platform_admin()`. `is_staff_user()` (verified def) returns true for owner/admin/manager/technician/sales/accounts/hr and FALSE for `viewer`. This exactly closes the reported defect (read-only viewer reading financial data) while preserving full read access for every role that the existing unscoped aggregation readers depend on. `is_platform_admin()` is retained explicitly because `is_staff_user()` only checks tenant `profiles.role` and would otherwise drop platform admins (tenant_id IS NULL) — though the RESTRICTIVE tenant-isolation policy already grants them; keeping it makes the SELECT self-consistent with the other expense policies' intent and harmless (both must pass: RESTRICTIVE AND PERMISSIVE).

WHY NOT submitter-scoping (the audit's 'ideal'): I traced every `from('expenses')` SELECT outside expensesService — financialReportsService.ts (P&L, 6 sites), caseFinanceService.ts (case profitability, 4 sites), ReportsDashboard.tsx (2 sites), featureGateService.ts (usage count), importExportService.ts (count). ALL run UNSCOPED tenant-wide aggregations with no `created_by` predicate and rely on seeing every row. A `created_by = auth.uid() OR has_role('manager'|'accounts')` RLS SELECT would silently undercount expenses in P&L, case margins, and reports for any technician/sales/hr user (who pass none of those role checks but legitimately view reports) — a correctness regression worse than the confidentiality leak it fixes. Submitter-scoping is therefore DEFERRED to a coordinated effort that first moves those aggregations to SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (overlaps EXP-056 stats RPC work) so the read path no longer depends on broad row visibility.

This is migration-only on the DB side; no app code must change for correctness. Optional defensive UI: hide the Expenses sidebar/route for `viewer` so they get a clean denial instead of an empty/erroring grid — but that is cosmetic and gated separately.

files.change: New migration (applied LIVE via MCP by the main loop, NOT by a subagent). DROP POLICY IF EXISTS expenses_select ON public.expenses; CREATE POLICY expenses_select AS PERMISSIVE FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (is_staff_user() OR is_platform_admin()). No table/column change. Keeps the RESTRICTIVE expenses_tenant_isolation policy untouched (still ANDed). Full SQL in migrationSql field. Must also be added to the migration manifest per CLAUDE.md migration discipline; no database.types.ts regen needed (policy-only change does not alter generated types, but run npm run db:types / the schema-drift check to confirm a no-op diff).

NO functional change required for correctness — fetchExpenses (line 121) and getExpenseStats (line 654) keep working for staff. Optional only: add a one-line comment above EXPENSE_LIST_COLUMNS or fetchExpenses noting 'visibility is gated at RLS to is_staff_user(); viewers cannot read expenses (EXP-053)'. Do not add a created_by predicate here — that would belong to the deferred submitter-scoping slice and would break nothing now but pre-empt a larger design.

OPTIONAL defensive UX (not required to close the finding). The list query (line 132) and stats query will simply return zero rows for a viewer under the new policy, which renders the existing EmptyState — functional but confusing. If polishing: guard the route/render with a role check (profile?.role !== 'viewer') or wire the Expenses sidebar entry through PermissionsContext.hasModuleAccess so viewers don't see the menu item. Keep this cosmetic and separate from the security migration; do NOT block the migration on it.

design.migrationSql:
-- EXP-053: expenses_select was USING(true) — every staff role incl. read-only
-- viewer could read tenant-wide expense financials. Gate SELECT behind staff
-- membership. is_staff_user() excludes 'viewer'; the RESTRICTIVE
-- expenses_tenant_isolation policy still ANDs tenant scoping on top of this.
-- is_platform_admin() retained for self-consistency with sibling policies
-- (harmless: PERMISSIVE OR RESTRICTIVE-AND semantics preserve isolation).

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS expenses_select ON public.expenses;

CREATE POLICY expenses_select
  ON public.expenses
  AS PERMISSIVE
  FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (is_staff_user() OR is_platform_admin());

-- Verification (run after apply):
-- SELECT polname, pg_get_expr(polqual, polrelid)
-- FROM pg_policy WHERE polrelid = 'public.expenses'::regclass AND polname = 'expenses_select';
-- expect: (is_staff_user() OR is_platform_admin())
design.tests: 1) DB-level RLS test (preferred — pgTAP-style or a scripted check via execute_sql with set-role/JWT impersonation in a NON-prod branch): as a tenant 'viewer' profile, SELECT count(*) FROM expenses returns 0; as an 'accounts'/'technician'/'manager'/'owner' profile in the same tenant, count(*) returns the full non-deleted tenant set (unchanged from before). Assert a viewer in tenant A and a staff user in tenant B still cannot read tenant A's rows (RESTRICTIVE isolation intact). 2) Reader-regression assertions (guard the blast radius): with a 'technician' principal, financialReportsService P&L expense total and caseFinanceService case-profitability expense sum equal the same values as an 'accounts' principal for the same date range/case (proves the staff aggregation readers did NOT lose rows). 3) Negative test: with a 'viewer' principal, getProfitLoss / getCaseFinancialSummary expense components are unaffected ONLY if viewers don't run them — document that viewers reading reports now see 0 expenses (acceptable: viewer is not in is_staff_user()); if any report surface is exposed to viewers, that is a separate follow-up. 4) Frontend: add/extend a test (e.g. ExpensesList or a service test) asserting fetchExpenses for a non-staff session yields [] without throwing (graceful empty state).
dependencies: Shares src/lib/expensesService.ts and src/pages/financial/ExpensesList.tsx with EXP-005/EXP-010/EXP-011/EXP-012/EXP-014/EXP-016/EXP-019/EXP-052 — but this slice changes ONLY a DB policy plus optional comments/UX, so the main loop can apply the migration independently; sequence any expensesService.ts text edits after the larger functional expense PRs to avoid conflicts.

DEFER submitter-scoped RLS (created_by) until the unscoped aggregation readers in financialReportsService.ts / caseFinanceService.ts / ReportsDashboard.tsx are moved behind SECURITY DEFINER RPCs — this overlaps EXP-056 (server-side get_expense_stats) and should be designed together.

EXP-011/EXP-012 (write-side least privilege: status-gate expenses_update, gate soft-delete to admin) are the ownership/write complement to this read-side fix; ordering-independent but both belong to the same 'expenses least-privilege' migration family noted in the audit batch at line 875.
design.blastRadius: What could break: any code path that SELECTs from `expenses` while authenticated as a NON-staff principal (only `viewer` today, since portal users hit different surfaces). After this change a viewer's expense queries return [] instead of rows. Verified readers and their exposure: ExpensesList grid+stats (viewer sees empty state — acceptable, that is the fix), financialReportsService P&L (financialReportsService.ts:98 and 5 more sites), caseFinanceService case profitability (caseFinanceService.ts:53 and 3 more), ReportsDashboard (lines 236,294), featureGateService usage count (line 267), importExportService count (line 561) — EVERY ONE of these runs as whatever staff role opened the page; none are viewer-only flows, and all staff roles remain inside is_staff_user(), so their aggregations return the identical row set as before. That is precisely why I rejected created_by-scoping: it WOULD have silently undercounted these aggregations for technician/sales/hr. Tenant isolation: untouched — the RESTRICTIVE expenses_tenant_isolation policy still ANDs `tenant_id = get_current_tenant_id() OR is_platform_admin()` on top, so cross-tenant reads remain impossible regardless of staff status. Invoices/VAT/banking: not touched — `expenses_select` governs only the expenses table; vat_records, financial_transactions, invoices, payments have their own policies (financial_transactions' own USING(true) is EXP-054, a separate finding). Writes (insert/update/delete) are unaffected — those policies already gate on has_role('accounts'/'admin'). The design avoids regression by choosing the one role-set (is_staff_user) that is the exact superset of every existing expense reader minus the one role the finding says must lose access (viewer), so no aggregation loses a row.

issues: VERIFIED LIVE: expenses_select is PERMISSIVE USING(true), ANDed only with RESTRICTIVE expenses_tenant_isolation ((tenant_id=get_current_tenant_id()) OR is_platform_admin()). The reported leak is real — any authenticated tenant principal incl. viewer reads all expense rows.

VERIFIED LIVE: is_staff_user() is SECURITY DEFINER, search_path=public, returns true for owner/admin/manager/technician/sales/accounts/hr and false for viewer. Fix logic is sound and closes the exact defect.

BLAST-RADIUS CLAIM IS INACCURATE (under-stated): is_staff_user() also gates on is_active=true AND deleted_at IS NULL, not role alone. The design repeatedly asserts viewer is 'the one role' / 'the exact superset minus viewer' that loses access — but ANY inactive or soft-deleted staff profile that could previously read via USING(true) will now get zero rows. Arguably correct, but it is an unstated behavior change the design must acknowledge.

Reader analysis is correct: financialReportsService/caseFinanceService/ReportsDashboard/featureGateService/importExportService all run unscoped as whatever staff role opened the page; all those roles remain inside is_staff_user(), so aggregations lose no rows. Rejecting created_by-scoping (which WOULD undercount technician/sales/hr report views) is the right call and correctly deferred to a SECURITY-DEFINER-RPC effort.

Anchors verified: fetchExpenses line 112, getExpenseStats line 645, isAccountsRole line 126, ExpensesList query line 132. is_platform_admin() in the new USING is harmless/redundant (RESTRICTIVE policy already grants platform admins) — fine to keep for self-consistency.

No duplicate work: shipped EXP-310/311 were write/ledger-side; sibling write policies (expenses_update=has_role('accounts'), expenses_delete=has_role('admin'), expenses_insert=USING(true)) are out of scope here and correctly deferred to EXP-011/012.

Migration safety: DROP+CREATE POLICY in one migration takes a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on expenses, instantaneous on a 1-row table. RESTRICTIVE isolation untouched — cross-tenant reads remain impossible. Reversible (recreate USING(true)). No column/type change; types regen is a no-op (run schema-drift check to confirm). Additive and safe.

Optional viewer route-guard is more than cosmetic: under the new policy a viewer's grid silently renders EmptyState, which is indistinguishable from real data loss / 'all expenses disappeared' and will generate support noise. Recommended to ship the PermissionsContext.hasModuleAccess gate alongside, though it should not block the migration.
mustFixBeforeShip: Correct the blast-radius/behavior narrative: state explicitly that is_staff_user() gates on is_active=true AND deleted_at IS NULL (not role alone), so inactive/soft-deleted staff also lose expense read access — do not claim viewer is the only affected principal.

Add a DB-level RLS regression test (set-role/JWT impersonation on a NON-prod branch) proving: viewer in tenant A reads 0 expense rows; accounts/technician/manager/owner in tenant A read the full non-deleted set unchanged; viewer in tenant A still cannot read tenant B (RESTRICTIVE intact). Do NOT run impersonation tests against the live DB.
verdict.migrationSafety: Safe and additive on the live DB. Policy-only DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE POLICY for SELECT; no table/column/data change, so the single existing expense row is untouched and no backfill is needed. RESTRICTIVE expenses_tenant_isolation is left intact, so multi-tenant isolation is preserved (both policies still AND). DROP POLICY briefly takes ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on expenses but is effectively instantaneous at this row count — negligible lock risk. Fully reversible by recreating USING(true). types regen is a no-op; run the schema-drift check to confirm. Must be applied LIVE by the main loop (not a subagent) and added to the migration manifest per CLAUDE.md.

item.title: financial_transactions ledger broadly visible/insertable (SELECT USING(true), INSERT is_staff_user())
item.scope: Tighten financial_transactions_insert to has_role('accounts') and narrow SELECT to at least is_staff_user(); platform-wide ledger surface, not expense-only.

design.title: Tighten financial_transactions RLS: INSERT to has_role('accounts'), SELECT to is_staff_user()
design.summary:
EXP-054 is a within-tenant least-privilege defect on the append-only ledger. Live policies (verified): `financial_transactions_tenant_isolation` RESTRICTIVE (correct, keep), `financial_transactions_select` PERMISSIVE `USING(true)`, `financial_transactions_insert` PERMISSIVE `WITH CHECK (is_staff_user())`. Tenant isolation is intact; the gap is (a) any role incl. `viewer` reads every posted ledger line tenant-wide, and (b) any staff (technician/sales/hr) can directly insert a ledger row.

Verified blast radius before tightening INSERT: every money-bearing write goes through a SECURITY DEFINER RPC (`post_manual_transaction`, `reverse_financial_transaction`, `record_payment`, `record_expense_disbursement`, `create_receipt_with_allocations`, `convert_proforma_to_tax_invoice` — all `prosecdef=true` confirmed). Under SECURITY DEFINER, RLS INSERT WITH CHECK is bypassed, so tightening `_insert` does NOT touch those flows. The ONLY app paths that perform a *direct* (non-definer) INSERT — and are therefore subject to the policy — are: (1) `createFinancialTransaction` in src/lib/financialService.ts, whose sole caller is `approveExpense` (src/lib/expensesService.ts:426); approval is already gated to accounts-or-higher in the UI, and `has_role('accounts')` resolves to exactly owner/admin/manager/accounts (verified from the function body) — so the new policy matches the existing authorization with zero behavior change for legitimate approvers; (2) the generic CSV importer for the `revenue`/`transactions` entity configs (src/lib/importExportService.ts:176-203) which inserts into `financial_transactions` directly — importing is an admin/accounts action, so accounts-gating is appropriate and consistent.

DESIGN: migration-only change, no TS code change required. (1) Drop+recreate `financial_transactions_insert` as `WITH CHECK (has_role('accounts'))`. (2) Drop+recreate `financial_transactions_select` as `USING (is_staff_user())` so viewers lose ledger read while all working staff keep it. Keep the RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation untouched (it ANDs with these). This is the audit's recommended minimum and is the safe full scope for EXP-054. Idempotency unique index (EXP-001/EXP-022) and ledger taxonomy/category (EXP-029) are explicitly OUT of scope here — separate items — and are noted as dependencies only for ordering.

files.path: supabase/migrations (applied via mcp__supabase__apply_migration by MAIN LOOP only)
files.change: New migration `tighten_financial_transactions_rls_exp054`. Body: (a) DROP POLICY IF EXISTS financial_transactions_insert ON public.financial_transactions; CREATE POLICY financial_transactions_insert ON public.financial_transactions AS PERMISSIVE FOR INSERT TO authenticated WITH CHECK (has_role('accounts')); (b) DROP POLICY IF EXISTS financial_transactions_select ON public.financial_transactions; CREATE POLICY financial_transactions_select ON public.financial_transactions AS PERMISSIVE FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (is_staff_user()); Do NOT touch financial_transactions_tenant_isolation (RESTRICTIVE) — it stays and ANDs with both. Full SQL in migrationSql field.

No edit needed for THIS change — RLS policy changes do not alter generated types (columns/functions unchanged). Manifest/types regen is only required if the project's schema-drift gate diffs policies; it does not. Leave untouched unless CI schema-drift flags it (it diffs types, not policies).

design.migrationSql:
-- EXP-054: tighten financial_transactions least-privilege (tenant isolation unchanged)
-- INSERT: only owner/admin/manager/accounts (has_role('accounts')) may directly insert a
-- ledger row. All money-bearing flows use SECURITY DEFINER RPCs and are unaffected; the only
-- direct-insert app paths are expense approval (already accounts-gated) and CSV import of
-- revenue/transactions (an accounts/admin action).
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS financial_transactions_insert ON public.financial_transactions;
CREATE POLICY financial_transactions_insert ON public.financial_transactions
  AS PERMISSIVE FOR INSERT TO authenticated
  WITH CHECK (has_role('accounts'));

-- SELECT: drop the open USING(true). Restrict to working staff (non-viewer) so read-only
-- viewers cannot read confidential ledger lines. ANDed with the RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation
-- policy, so cross-tenant reads remain impossible.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS financial_transactions_select ON public.financial_transactions;
CREATE POLICY financial_transactions_select ON public.financial_transactions
  AS PERMISSIVE FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (is_staff_user());

-- financial_transactions_tenant_isolation (RESTRICTIVE FOR ALL) is intentionally left intact.
design.tests:
Add a SQL-level RLS regression (executed by MAIN LOOP via mcp__supabase__execute_sql against a scratch tenant, not committed as app code unless an RLS test harness exists):
1. As a `viewer` JWT for tenant T: SELECT from financial_transactions returns 0 rows (previously returned T's rows) — asserts EXP-054 SELECT tightening.
2. As an `accounts` (or owner/admin/manager) JWT for T: SELECT returns T's ledger rows — asserts staff still read.
3. As a `technician`/`sales`/`hr` JWT: direct INSERT into financial_transactions is rejected by RLS (WITH CHECK fails) — asserts INSERT tightening.
4. As an `accounts` JWT: direct INSERT with valid tenant_id succeeds — asserts approval/import path still works.
5. Regression for SECURITY DEFINER bypass: as a `technician` JWT, calling `post_manual_transaction(...)` / `record_payment(...)` still posts a ledger row (definer bypasses the new INSERT policy) — confirms no collateral breakage of RPC flows.
6. Cross-tenant: as accounts of tenant T2, SELECT returns 0 rows for T1's data (RESTRICTIVE isolation still holds).
App-level: extend src/lib/expensesService.test.ts to assert `approveExpense` still calls `createFinancialTransaction` (no behavior change) — the mock already exists at line 8; this guards that the design did not require a code path change.
dependencies: EXP-001/EXP-022 (partial unique index on financial_transactions(reference_type, reference_id) WHERE reference_type='expense' AND deleted_at IS NULL) — the audit lists the index alongside EXP-054 but it is a SEPARATE migration; sequence it independently, do not bundle here.
EXP-029 (ledger category_id taxonomy map master_transaction_categories) — independent; not required for this RLS change.

No shared edits with src/lib/expensesService.ts or src/pages/financial/ExpensesList.tsx — this item is migration-only, so it does NOT contend with the other expense items the main loop is sequencing on those files; it can land in any order relative to them.
design.blastRadius: Could-break analysis, each mitigated: (1) Expense approval — `approveExpense`->`createFinancialTransaction` is the only non-definer ledger INSERT in app code; it is invoked only by accounts-or-higher (UI gate isAccountsRole) and `has_role('accounts')`=owner/admin/manager/accounts, so legitimate approvals are unaffected; a non-accounts user who somehow reached it now fails at the DB (correct hardening). (2) CSV import of revenue/transactions (importExportService entity configs at :176-203) does a direct insert — importing is an accounts/admin operation, so accounts-gating is consistent; a non-accounts importer would now be blocked at the ledger (acceptable, matches intent). (3) Invoices/payments/receipts/banking/disbursement — ALL go through SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (verified prosecdef=true for post_manual_transaction, reverse_financial_transaction, record_payment, record_expense_disbursement, create_receipt_with_allocations, convert_proforma_to_tax_invoice); RLS INSERT WITH CHECK is bypassed under definer, so these are untouched. (4) Reports/reads — financialReportsService, transactionsService.getTransactionStats, TransactionsList, RevenueDashboard all run as working staff (non-viewer accountants/managers) and keep SELECT; only `viewer` loses ledger read, which is the intended confidentiality fix. (5) Tenant isolation — the RESTRICTIVE policy is left intact and ANDs with the new permissive policies, so no cross-tenant exposure is introduced. (6) Platform admin — both is_staff_user() and the isolation policy already special-case is_platform_admin via the RESTRICTIVE clause; platform admins are unaffected. VAT/invoices have no direct dependency on these policies. Reversibility: pure policy swap, instantly revertible by recreating USING(true)/is_staff_user() if an unforeseen non-definer staff insert path surfaces.

issues: VERIFIED CORRECT: Live policies match the design's stated baseline exactly — financial_transactions_insert WITH CHECK is_staff_user(), financial_transactions_select USING(true), financial_transactions_tenant_isolation RESTRICTIVE FOR ALL ((tenant_id = get_current_tenant_id()) OR is_platform_admin()).

VERIFIED CORRECT: All 6 money RPCs are prosecdef=true (post_manual_transaction, reverse_financial_transaction, record_payment, record_expense_disbursement, create_receipt_with_allocations, convert_proforma_to_tax_invoice). Under SECURITY DEFINER the INSERT WITH CHECK is bypassed, so tightening _insert does not affect those flows.

VERIFIED CORRECT: has_role('accounts') resolves to owner/admin/manager/accounts (confirmed from function body); is_staff_user() = all 7 non-viewer roles. So the new INSERT policy is strictly narrower than the old one and matches approveExpense's existing accounts-gating.

VERIFIED CORRECT: Only two non-definer direct INSERT paths exist. (1) createFinancialTransaction (financialService.ts:33-60, plain .insert) whose sole caller is approveExpense (expensesService.ts:426) which already enforces SoD (creator!=approver). (2) generic CSV importer at ImportWizard.tsx:259-260 doing supabase.from(config.tableName).insert(...) for the revenue/transactions entity configs (importExportService.ts:179,192). Both confirmed in code.

BEHAVIOR-CHANGE OVERSTATEMENT: design claims 'zero behavior change' and 'no TS code change required'. The CSV-import path IS a behavior change for technician/sales/hr: today is_staff_user() lets them import revenue/transactions; after the migration the DB rejects their insert. ImportWizard surfaces the raw insert error (no accounts-only UI gate on ImportExport.tsx), so they get an opaque failure mid-import, not a clean 'not permitted' message.

PARTIAL-BATCH RISK (pre-existing, not introduced): ImportWizard.insert(recordsToInsert) is not transaction-wrapped; a blocked non-accounts importer could leave a partially-applied batch. Pre-existing importer behavior but newly reachable as a failure mode for non-accounts staff on the ledger entity.

SELECT tightening is safe: transactionsService/financialReports readers run as working staff (non-viewer); only viewer loses ledger read, which is the intended confidentiality fix. Cross-tenant reads remain blocked by the untouched RESTRICTIVE policy.

Migration is additive-equivalent (policy DROP IF EXISTS + CREATE), instantly reversible, no table rewrite, brief catalog-level lock only. No database.types.ts regen required (RLS policies do not alter generated types). Not duplicate work — distinct from EXP-001/022 index and EXP-029 taxonomy.
mustFixBeforeShip: Correct the design's 'zero behavior change' claim: acknowledge that non-accounts staff (technician/sales/hr) lose the ability to CSV-import revenue/transactions, and that this surfaces as a raw RLS insert error in ImportWizard. Either (a) accept and document it as intended hardening, or (b) add a lightweight UI gate so the importer hides/blocks the revenue & transactions entities for non-accounts users (PermissionsContext) to avoid an opaque mid-import failure — this touches ImportExport.tsx/ImportWizard.tsx, so the main loop must sequence it against other import edits.

Keep the RLS regression tests in the design, but add an explicit assertion that a technician/sales/hr direct INSERT into financial_transactions is rejected AND that the same role calling the SECURITY DEFINER RPCs still posts a row (the design lists this as test 5 — ensure it actually runs against a scratch tenant before the policy lands on the live DB).
verdict.migrationSafety: Safe and additive-equivalent. The migration only swaps two PERMISSIVE policies (DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE) and explicitly leaves the RESTRICTIVE financial_transactions_tenant_isolation intact, so cross-tenant isolation is preserved (verified live: RESTRICTIVE FOR ALL with tenant_id = get_current_tenant_id() OR is_platform_admin()). No data rows touched, no column/type change, no DROP TABLE/COLUMN, no DELETE. Lock is a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on pg_policy for the relation only — negligible on a 1-row table. Fully reversible by recreating USING(true)/is_staff_user(). New policies tighten (narrower than current), so they cannot grant access that did not exist. No database.types.ts regen needed. One residual: there is a tiny window where, between DROP and CREATE inside the same migration txn, the policy is absent — but since DDL runs in a transaction the change is atomic, so no exposure. The only real-world consequence is the intended one: viewers lose SELECT and non-accounts staff lose direct INSERT (incl. the CSV import path), which is a behavior change to flag, not a safety defect.

item.title: Bulk import bypasses max_expenses_per_month and bulk_import plan gates (UI-only enforcement)
item.scope: Enforce plan limits server-side (RPC/RLS-callable checking subscription + monthly count before insert); gate the Import wizard on hasFeature('bulk_import')+checkUsageLimit.

design.title: Server-enforced bulk-import gating for expenses (bulk_import feature + max_expenses_per_month quota)
design.summary:
The expense bulk-import path has zero feature/usage enforcement. The ImportWizard (`src/components/importExport/ImportWizard.tsx` handleImport, lines 174-293) does a direct `supabase.from('expenses').insert(batch)` per 100 rows and never calls hasFeature/checkUsageLimit/canPerformAction. The only gate that exists is the UI-only `UsageLimitGuard` wrapping the create button in `ExpenseFormModal.tsx:427`. Live DB confirms `expenses_insert` RLS = `has_role('accounts')` only (no plan check), no `create_expense`/limit RPC exists, and there are ZERO `plan_features` rows for `bulk_import` or `max_expenses_per_month` — so today the monthly cap resolves to null (unlimited) and `bulk_import` resolves only via the client-side FEATURE_REQUIREMENTS hierarchy (requires 'professional'). A Starter tenant can load thousands of expenses via Import, bypassing both gates and any future per-month cap.

Durable fix = a single server-side chokepoint, additive and independent of EXP-031 (which is not shipped). Add one SECURITY DEFINER RPC, `assert_expense_import_allowed(p_row_count int)`, that: (1) authorizes via `has_role('accounts')` (RLS is bypassed under definer); (2) resolves the caller's active plan + the `bulk_import` feature flag + the `max_expenses_per_month` limit from `tenant_subscriptions`/`plan_features` using the SAME resolution rules the client uses (explicit plan_features row wins; else feature-hierarchy fallback for bulk_import requiring 'professional'; else null=unlimited for the limit); (3) counts this tenant's current-calendar-month expenses (NOT deleted) and RAISES EXCEPTION with a stable error code/message if either the feature is missing or current+p_row_count would exceed the limit. The ImportWizard calls this RPC once before the insert loop and aborts the whole import on failure. The ImportExport page additionally hides/disables the Expenses Import entry behind `hasFeature('bulk_import')` for a clean UX (defense-in-depth, not the authority). This is the shared server-side enforcement the audit calls out for EXP-063/EXP-071; it does not touch the create/edit/approve write paths, invoices, VAT, banking, or reports.

Note: import is generic across ~12 entity types but only Expenses has these specific gates; the RPC and the UI gate are expense-scoped, so other entity imports are untouched.

files.change: Gate the expense import on the server RPC before any insert. In handleImport (currently lines 174-293), at the very top of the try block — after `const { supabase } = await import('../../lib/supabaseClient');` and computing `validRows` — add an expense-only preflight: `if (entityType === 'expenses') { const { error: gateErr } = await supabase.rpc('assert_expense_import_allowed', { p_row_count: validRows.length }); if (gateErr) { logger.error('Expense import gate denied:', gateErr); setImportResult({ success: 0, errors: parsedData.length }); setIsImporting(false); return; } }`. Also surface the message to the user: capture `gateErr.message` into a new piece of state `const [gateError, setGateError] = useState<string | null>(null);` (declare near the other useState hooks ~line 45-55), set it in the failure branch, and render it in the Step 4 block (the `!isImporting` branch, ~lines 744-757) as a danger-token banner (`bg-danger-muted border border-danger/30 text-danger`) when `gateError` is set, replacing the success copy. Keep the RPC name as a literal string — `assert_expense_import_allowed` will exist in the regenerated database.types.ts Functions map so `supabase.rpc(...)` is typed; if tsc complains before regen, that is expected and resolves after the main loop regenerates types. Do NOT change the generic insert path for other entity types.

Add a defense-in-depth UI gate so a tenant without bulk_import can't even open the Expenses import wizard. Import `hasFeature` from '../../lib/featureGateService'. Add a query: `const { data: canBulkImport = true } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['feature','bulk_import'], queryFn: () => hasFeature('bulk_import') });`. In handleEntitySelect (lines 61-68), when `action === 'import'`, guard: `if (!canBulkImport) { /* show upgrade toast via useToast */ return; }` — add `import { useToast } from '../../hooks/useToast'` and `const toast = useToast();`, call `toast.error('Bulk import requires the Professional plan. Please upgrade.')` then return. Optionally disable the Import button (line 186-193) with `disabled={!canBulkImport}` and a tooltip title. Leave Export untouched. This is UX only — the RPC is the authority.

OPTIONAL convenience (only if the wizard or page wants a single client preflight that mirrors the server). Add `export async function canBulkImportExpenses(rowCount: number): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; message?: string }>` that runs `const feat = await checkFeatureAccess('bulk_import'); if (!feat.allowed) return { allowed:false, message: 'Bulk import requires the '+(feat.requiredPlan??'Professional')+' plan.' }; return canPerformAction('max_expenses_per_month', rowCount);`. This keeps the UI message consistent with the server. Pure addition, no change to existing exports. If you prefer minimal surface, skip this and inline hasFeature in ImportExport.tsx as described above.

design.tests:
Add tests at src/lib/__tests__/expenseImportGate.test.ts (or extend the existing import/feature-gate test file if one exists):
1. ImportWizard preflight unit test (mock supabase.rpc): when entityType==='expenses', handleImport calls supabase.rpc('assert_expense_import_allowed', { p_row_count: <validRows.length> }) exactly once BEFORE any .from('expenses').insert; assert insert is never called when rpc returns { error }.
2. Gate-denied UX: when the rpc resolves with error.message set, importResult.errors === parsedData.length, importResult.success === 0, and the gateError banner text is rendered.
3. Non-expense entity (e.g. 'customers') import does NOT call assert_expense_import_allowed (rpc not invoked with that name) — proves scoping.
4. ImportExport page: with hasFeature('bulk_import') mocked false, clicking the Expenses Import button does not open the wizard (showImportWizard stays false) and a toast.error fires; with true, the wizard opens.
DB-level (document as SQL assertions for the main loop to run post-migration, since subagents can't apply migrations): as an 'accounts' user on a starter tenant with a plan_features row max_expenses_per_month=50 and current month count=48, `select assert_expense_import_allowed(5)` RAISES (would hit 53>50); `select assert_expense_import_allowed(2)` succeeds (50). With bulk_import is_enabled=false row, any positive count RAISES with HINT 'feature:bulk_import'. As a non-accounts user it RAISES 42501. With no max_expenses_per_month row it returns void (unlimited).
dependencies: Shares src/components/importExport/ImportWizard.tsx with EXP-062/EXP-064/EXP-065/EXP-067/EXP-068 (import-integrity items) and src/pages/settings/ImportExport.tsx — main loop must sequence edits to this wizard to avoid conflicts; the gate preflight is a self-contained block at the top of handleImport and merges cleanly with those changes.

Does NOT depend on EXP-031 (atomic create_expense RPC) — intentionally independent so it ships without the keystone. If EXP-031/PR-1 later routes import through create_expense, this assert can be called once up-front OR folded into a bulk RPC; keep it as a standalone guard for now.

Shares the gating concern with EXP-071 (edit-then-submit create-path bypass): the durable server-side enforcement pattern is the same. EXP-071's create/submit path is a separate chokepoint (ExpenseFormModal / future submit_expense RPC) and is out of scope here; note the shared intent so they converge on server authority.
Touches src/lib/featureGateService.ts only if the optional canBulkImportExpenses helper is added — disjoint from expensesService.ts, so no conflict with other expense items.
Requires database.types.ts regeneration (main loop, via MCP) after the migration so supabase.rpc('assert_expense_import_allowed') is typed; until then tsc may flag the rpc name.
design.blastRadius: Scope is tight. The new RPC is additive (no signature/behavior change to any existing function) and is invoked only from the expense branch of the import wizard, so invoices, VAT, banking, payments, reports, and other entity imports are untouched — verified that the import insert path and the financial write paths are separate. RLS is unchanged (expenses_insert stays has_role('accounts')); the RPC adds enforcement on top, it does not relax anything. Because the function is SECURITY DEFINER, RLS is bypassed inside it, so it authorizes explicitly with has_role('accounts') and scopes every read to get_current_tenant_id() — preserving tenant isolation. Behavioral-parity risk: the audit's live-DB reality is that plan_features has NO rows for these two keys, so today the gate is effectively open (bulk_import via hierarchy=professional, limit=unlimited). The RPC deliberately mirrors that exact resolution (row-wins, else hierarchy, else unlimited) so it will NOT suddenly start blocking tenants who weren't blocked before — it only enforces once operators seed plan_features (which is the intended billing config). One subtle divergence from the client by design: the server counts NON-deleted expenses (`deleted_at IS NULL`), whereas the client checkUsageLimit('max_expenses_per_month') currently counts ALL rows including soft-deleted; the server is the more correct metering basis and is the authority — flag this so the client count can be aligned later (not in this PR, to keep blast radius small). Failure mode is safe: if the RPC errors for any reason the wizard aborts the import (no partial silent inserts). The only user-visible change for a correctly-entitled tenant is none; for an under-entitled tenant the import is now refused with a clear upgrade message instead of silently succeeding.

verdict.migrationSafety: Migration is SAFE and additive. It only does CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION + REVOKE/GRANT/COMMENT — no DDL on tables, no data writes, no locks beyond a momentary catalog lock on the function. Verified the function name assert_expense_import_allowed does NOT already exist, so no clobbering. SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path=public is correct; it authorizes explicitly via has_role('accounts') (all three referenced functions has_role(text)/get_current_tenant_id()/is_platform_admin() exist live) and scopes every read to get_current_tenant_id(), preserving RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation. Fully reversible via DROP FUNCTION. Live-DB reality confirms the design's safety claim: tenant_subscriptions has 0 rows (all tenants fall back to 'starter') and plan_features has 0 rows for both bulk_import and max_expenses_per_month, so the RPC resolves bulk_import via hierarchy=professional and limit=unlimited — meaning the gate stays effectively open until operators seed plan_features, so no tenant who imports today suddenly breaks. Touches NONE of financial_transactions/VAT/invoices/banking/reports. One reversibility caveat: REVOKE ALL ... FROM public then GRANT TO authenticated is fine, but if a prior grant existed it would be reset — moot here since the function is brand new.
issues: NOT true insert-path enforcement: the RPC is a wizard preflight only. expenses_insert RLS stays has_role('accounts') with no plan check, so a direct supabase-js/API caller (or any non-wizard insert path) bypasses the gate entirely. The summary overstates this as 'server-side enforcement'/'the authority' — it is server-side COMPUTATION invoked from one client path, not a constraint on writes. Acceptable for the stated scope but must be labeled honestly.

Non-atomic / TOCTOU: gate checks validRows.length once before the batch loop; concurrent imports or the partial-success loop (batch errors increment errorCount but the loop keeps inserting subsequent batches) can push the month count past the quota. Soft gate only.

Resolution divergence from client (by design but undocumented in code): RPC filters tenant_subscriptions.deleted_at IS NULL and plan_features.deleted_at IS NULL; client loadPlanCache (featureGateService.ts L89-100, L112-115) filters NEITHER — it keys only on status='active' and plan_id. Zero impact today (0 rows) but the two will diverge once soft-deleted plan rows exist.

Month-boundary timezone mismatch: client computes startOfMonth in BROWSER-LOCAL time (checkUsageLimit L262-264) then toISOString(); RPC uses date_trunc('month', now()) in server UTC. For GMT+4 tenants these differ by up to ~a day at month edges, so client preview and server gate can disagree on the boundary. Cosmetic for metering.

ImportExport.tsx 'optional' suggestion to add disabled={!canBulkImport} on the Import button (L186-193) is WRONG as written: that button is shared across all ~12 entity types in the .map(), so it would disable Import for customers/invoices/etc., not just expenses. Only the handleEntitySelect guard (L61-68) is correctly expense-scoped — but note that guard as proposed ('if (!canBulkImport) return') also blocks ALL entities, not just expenses; it must additionally check entityType==='expenses'.

Server counts deleted_at IS NULL expenses; client checkUsageLimit('max_expenses_per_month') counts ALL rows incl. soft-deleted (no deleted_at filter, L266-270). Design flags this as a deliberate, deferred divergence — acceptable but the client preflight message can disagree with the server.

tsc will break until types regen: supabase.rpc('assert_expense_import_allowed', ...) is not in the Functions map yet. Design acknowledges this; main loop must regen database.types.ts via MCP after applying the migration, or the typecheck CI gate fails.
mustFixBeforeShip: Scope the ImportExport.tsx UI gate to expenses ONLY: in handleEntitySelect, guard with `if (action==='import' && entityType==='expenses' && !canBulkImport) { toast.error(...); return; }`. Do NOT add disabled={!canBulkImport} to the shared Import button (L186-193) — it would disable import for every entity type. Verify the useToast import path ('../../hooks/useToast') actually exists before wiring it.

Reword the summary/PR claim from 'server-side enforcement of the insert path / the authority' to 'wizard-scoped server preflight'. The expenses_insert RLS is unchanged, so direct API inserts still bypass this. If true enforcement is required, that belongs on the insert path (RLS WITH CHECK or a create/bulk RPC under EXP-031) — out of scope here, but state the gap so it isn't mistaken for closed.

Align the RPC's resolution with the live client semantics OR add an explicit code comment that the deleted_at filters on tenant_subscriptions/plan_features are an intentional, stricter divergence (client filters neither). Pick one and document it so the next reviewer doesn't treat it as a parity bug.

After applying the migration, regenerate src/types/database.types.ts via MCP in the SAME PR so supabase.rpc('assert_expense_import_allowed') typechecks; otherwise the typecheck CI gate blocks the merge.

item.title: Import doesn't stamp created_by and never validates category_id/case_id existence/tenancy; whole-batch failures
item.scope: Add referenceFields existence/tenancy validation in validateField, switch to per-row insert/error capture, explicitly stamp created_by (or require submitted_by mapping).

design.title: Import: stamp created_by (DB trigger), validate category_id/case_id existence + tenancy, and surface per-row errors instead of whole-batch failure
design.summary:
Grounded in the live code/DB, EXP-064 has three independent defects, each fixed at the right layer:

1) created_by is NULL on imported expenses. The only BEFORE trigger on `expenses` is `set_expenses_tenant_and_audit` → `set_tenant_and_audit_fields()`, which stamps tenant_id/created_at/updated_at but NOT created_by (verified via pg_get_functiondef). The CLAUDE.md note that `set_audit_actor_fields()` covers expenses is inaccurate — that trigger is NOT attached to `expenses`. The ImportWizard also never sends created_by (records are built solely from `fieldMappings` in handleImport, ImportWizard.tsx:196-243). FIX (DB, robust, single chokepoint): add a BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE trigger `set_expenses_audit_actor` running a SECURITY DEFINER fn that does `NEW.created_by := COALESCE(NEW.created_by, auth.uid())` on INSERT and `NEW.updated_by := COALESCE(auth.uid(), NEW.updated_by)` on UPDATE. This makes EVERY write path (import, form createExpense, future RPCs) attribute the submitter without touching any caller. (Aligns with PR-7's EXP-010 actor-stamping intent; I scope it to the additive trigger only so it is safe to ship inside the import PR.)

2) No reference existence/tenancy validation. `validateField` (importExportService.ts:888-1005) never checks `referenceFields`, so a bogus/wrong-tenant category_id/case_id passes and FK-fails at insert. master_expense_categories is GLOBAL (no tenant_id; has is_active, no deleted_at). cases is TENANT-SCOPED (has deleted_at; RLS already restricts SELECT to current tenant). FIX (frontend, async): add `validateExpenseReferences(rows, mappings)` to importExportService that batches DISTINCT mapped category_id/case_id values and checks them — category_id against master_expense_categories (exists AND is_active), case_id against cases (exists AND deleted_at IS NULL; RLS makes a wrong-tenant id resolve to "not found", so this doubles as the tenancy check). Wire it into ImportWizard.handleValidate so bad references become per-row validation errors (excluded from import, listed in the errors panel/CSV) BEFORE any insert. Mirrors the existing bulkLookupNames batching pattern.

3) Whole-batch failure on one bad FK. handleImport does `errorCount += batch.length` on any batch error (ImportWizard.tsx:272). FIX (frontend): keep the fast bulk insert as the happy path, but on batch error fall back to per-row inserts for that batch, counting successes/failures per row and capturing each failed row's DB message into importResult so 99 good rows still land and the operator sees which row failed.

Safe-slice boundaries (explicitly deferred to their own findings, not regressed here): currency/exchange_rate/amount_base snapshot on import = EXP-062 (separate); ledger/VAT posting on import = EXP-031; bulk_import/usage gating = EXP-063; phantom paid_at + expense_number unique index = EXP-065; import job/log provenance rows = EXP-067; number/date coercion = EXP-068. This package does NOT route import through createExpense (that is EXP-031/EXP-062's atomic-RPC scope) to keep blast radius contained.

files.path: supabase/migrations (apply via MCP by main loop)
files.change: NEW migration `expenses_audit_actor_stamping`. (a) CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.set_expenses_audit_actor() RETURNS trigger LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path TO 'public' that on TG_OP='INSERT' sets NEW.created_by := COALESCE(NEW.created_by, auth.uid()) and on 'UPDATE' sets NEW.updated_by := COALESCE(auth.uid(), NEW.updated_by). (b) DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_expenses_audit_actor ON public.expenses; CREATE TRIGGER set_expenses_audit_actor BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON public.expenses FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.set_expenses_audit_actor(). Do NOT touch the existing set_expenses_tenant_and_audit trigger (it runs alongside; both are BEFORE row triggers, order is alphabetical — actor before tenant, both idempotent so order-independent). No data backfill in this migration (historical NULL created_by left as-is; optional backfill is out of scope and would need a known actor). After apply: regenerate src/types/database.types.ts via mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types (no column shape change expected, but run the schema-drift gate).

Add a new exported async function `validateExpenseReferences(rows: Record<string,any>[], mappings: Record<string,string>): Promise<ValidationResult[]>` (place near validateField). Logic: build reverse map of target→source from `mappings`; collect DISTINCT non-empty values for target fields category_id and case_id across rows. If any category_id values: `supabase.from('master_expense_categories').select('id').in('id', ids)` (and filter is_active: also select is_active, treat inactive as invalid) → build Set of valid ids. If any case_id values: `supabase.from('cases').select('id').in('id', ids).is('deleted_at', null)` → Set of valid ids (RLS scopes to tenant, so out-of-tenant ids are absent = invalid, giving the tenancy check for free). Then iterate rows; for each row whose mapped category_id/case_id is non-empty but not in the valid Set, push a ValidationResult {rowNumber: index+2, field, error: 'category_id does not exist or is inactive' / 'case_id does not exist or is not in this tenant', rowData: row}. design.blastRadius: What could break and how it's avoided: (1) The new created_by trigger fires on ALL expenses writes, including the form createExpense path. It only sets created_by when NULL (COALESCE), so the form path — which doesn't pass created_by — gains correct attribution and existing rows that DO pass it are untouched. updated_by uses COALESCE(auth.uid(), NEW.updated_by) so system/bypass updates don't wipe it. Both this trigger and set_expenses_tenant_and_audit are BEFORE-row and idempotent, so coexistence is safe regardless of fire order. No invoice/VAT/banking/report path touches expenses triggers. (2) Reference validation is gated on entityType==='expenses' and runs only in the wizard's validate step — zero effect on cases/invoices/inventory/other imports or on any export path. The cases lookup relies on existing RLS for tenancy (no new policy), so it cannot leak cross-tenant rows. (3) Per-row fallback only activates when a bulk batch ALREADY errored (today that whole batch is lost anyway), so clean imports and all other entity types keep the fast single bulk insert — no performance regression and no behavior change on the success path. (4) financial_transactions is untouched (no ledger posting added here — deferred to EXP-031), so the append-only/reverse-only invariant is not engaged. (5) No VAT path touched, so EXP-032's shared calculateVATForPeriod / sales vat_records risk is entirely out of scope for this item. (6) Soft-delete respected: case_id validation filters deleted_at IS NULL; no hard deletes introduced.

issues: DB diagnosis is fully verified and correct: created_by is NULL on imports because set_tenant_and_audit_fields() does not stamp it and the ImportWizard never sends it. The proposed actor trigger is the right single-chokepoint fix.

master_expense_categories confirmed GLOBAL (id + is_active, no deleted_at, no tenant_id); cases confirmed tenant-scoped (id + deleted_at + tenant_id). The 'RLS gives tenancy check for free' framing is valid for case_id ONLY; a global category has no tenant dimension so is_active is the only meaningful filter there. Design states this correctly.

Mapping-direction hazard the design underspecifies: ImportWizard rows are keyed by SOURCE CSV headers and fieldMappings is {source:target}. validateExpenseReferences must resolve row[source] per mapped target (category_id/case_id) and skip unmapped targets. The 'reverse map target->source' direction is right but easy to invert; mirror the existing validateField loop (lines 153-167).

Completeness gap that undercuts defect #3's headline value: surfacing per-row DB error messages is left 'optional', but Step-4 UI (lines 726-757) renders only success/errors COUNTS with no surface for failing-row messages. As written the operator gets corrected counts but NOT 'which row failed'.
Per-row fallback is sound and only triggers after a bulk batch already errored (today that batch is fully lost), so no perf regression on clean files and no behavior change for other entity types.

No duplication of shipped work: no actor trigger exists on expenses yet; EXP-010 (obs 8720) is still a pending finding. Trigger only sets created_by/updated_by when null (COALESCE) so the form createExpense path gains attribution and no invoice/quote/VAT/banking/report path is touched.

verdict.migrationSafety: SAFE and additive. Verified live: the only BEFORE trigger on public.expenses is set_expenses_tenant_and_audit -> set_tenant_and_audit_fields(), whose body (confirmed via pg_get_functiondef) stamps tenant_id/created_at/updated_at but NOT created_by/updated_by; set_audit_actor_fields is NOT attached to expenses (CLAUDE.md note is inaccurate, as the design states). The migration only CREATE OR REPLACE a SECURITY DEFINER fn (SET search_path=public) and DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS + CREATE TRIGGER — fully re-runnable, no DROP/DELETE, no backfill, no column shape change. Lock concern is only the brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE that CREATE TRIGGER takes on expenses; with ~1 row this is negligible. RESTRICTIVE tenant isolation untouched; financial_transactions append-only invariant not engaged (ledger posting correctly deferred to EXP-031). auth.uid() resolves under SECURITY DEFINER (same pattern as existing fn); COALESCE preserves explicitly-passed created_by and never wipes updated_by. Reversible by dropping the trigger. Sequencing caveat: EXP-010 also targets expenses actor-stamping — main loop must ship only one actor trigger.
